channel.title: Paper Feeds (arXiv)
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv

item.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13621v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究定位：从运行时防护到可防御性分析

本文挑战“屏蔽（shield）仅是强化学习中运行时安全约束”的主流范式，提出屏蔽综合（shield synthesis）本质上是一种设计阶段的可防御性分析框架。我们重构了传统基于自动机的时序逻辑编译流程——包括规格编译、乘积博弈构建、吸引子计算与获胜区提取——将其解读为揭示系统内在结构特性的分析工具，而非仅生成部署时的动作过滤器。

方法创新：不对称双规格安全博弈建模

针对网络防御场景，我们构建了一个受约束的双人安全博弈：

  • 防御方规格定义系统的不可达（unsafe）状态集（即需绝对避免的拓扑失效模式）；
  • 攻击方规格则在吸引子计算过程中限制对手的合法行动空间（反映现实中的能力约束，如渗透路径限制），实现规格的不对称施加。

求解该博弈输出三项核心产物：可防御性裁决（defensibility verdict）（形式化判定某网络拓扑是否可在给定规格下被完全防御）、获胜区域（winning region）（所有初始状态下防御者必胜的状态子集）及对应的屏蔽策略。

关键发现：可防御性指纹与架构敏感性

超越二元裁决，我们从吸引子层级结构中提取拓扑级度量（如最小防御覆盖半径、吸引子深度分布），并融合屏蔽约束下的对抗性多智能体RL收敛行为（如攻防交互频率、策略僵持周期），构建可防御性指纹（defensibility fingerprint）——同时刻画系统的形式安全性与适应性对抗下的操作韧性。
“假设分析”表明：形式可防御性与操作有效性表征安全的不同维度——微小架构调整（如增加一条冗余链路）可能引发操作性能的剧烈波动（如平均攻破时间下降70%），而形式获胜区面积变化却不足5%。这证实屏蔽综合的核心价值不在于生成安全策略，而在于**回答根本性架构问题：系统能否被防御？何处最脆弱？如何重构以提升鲁棒性？** 可防御性裁决本身，才是真正的分析输出。

AI Summary (English)

This paper repositions shield synthesis—not as a runtime enforcement mechanism for safe RL agents, but as a design-time defensibility analysis framework. We reinterpret the standard automata-theoretic pipeline (specification compilation, product game construction, attractor computation, winning-region extraction) as yielding structural insights about system architecture rather than just executable constraints. For network defense, we formalize an asymmetric two-player safety game: the defender’s specification defines the unsafe region, while the attacker’s specification constrains adversarial actions only during attractor computation, enabling realistic capability modeling. Solving the game produces a formal defensibility verdict, the associated winning region, and a shield—constituting a certificate of architectural safety. Beyond binary verdicts, we derive topology-level metrics from attractor structure and integrate them with post-convergence behavior from shield-constrained adversarial multi-agent RL to form a defensibility fingerprint, jointly capturing formal safety and operational resilience under adaptive attack. Crucially, “what-if” analysis reveals that formal defensibility and operational effectiveness are decoupled: minor architectural changes can drastically alter operational outcomes while leaving formal safety margins nearly invariant. Thus, shield synthesis is most valuable as a diagnostic tool for architectural security questions—not as a deployment shield.

Abstract

Shielded reinforcement learning is typically presented as a runtime safety mechanism that compiles temporal-logic specifications into automata restricting an agent's actions. We argue this is the wrong product. The same automata-theoretic machinery -- specification compilation, product game construction, attractor computation, and winning-region extraction -- is better read as a design-time analytical instrument whose outputs are structural insights about a system rather than runtime constraints on a deployed agent. We instantiate this through a constrained two-player safety game for network defense. The two specifications are enforced asymmetrically: the defender specification defines the unsafe region of the game, whereas the attacker specification restricts the adversary's legal actions during attractor computation. Solving the game yields a defensibility verdict -- a formal certificate that a topology-specification pair is or is not defensible -- with the associated winning region and shield. Beyond the binary verdict, we derive topology-level metrics from the attractor structure and combine them with post-convergence behavior from shield-constrained adversarial multi-agent reinforcement learning. Together these form a defensibility fingerprint capturing both a network's formal safety properties and its operational behavior under adaptive play. A what-if analysis shows that formal defensibility and operational effectiveness capture distinct aspects of security: small architectural changes can produce large shifts in operational outcomes while leaving formal safety margins nearly unchanged. Shield synthesis is thus most valuable not as a deployment mechanism for safe agents, but as a framework for answering architectural questions about whether, where, and how a system can be defended. The defensibility verdict is the output, not the safe policy.

item.title: Who Pays the Price? Stakeholder-Centric Prompt Injection Benchmarking for Real-world Web Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13385v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）驱动的Web智能体日益部署于真实网络环境，其需直接解析不可信网页内容并执行具实际后果的操作（如下单、支付、信息提交），由此面临严峻的提示注入（prompt injection）攻击风险。现有安全评测基准多采用攻击中心视角，仅关注注入技术可行性，却忽视了攻击后果在不同利益相关方（如用户、商家、平台）间的非对称性分布——同一注入可能对用户造成隐私泄露，却使平台误收虚假订单，而对卖家产生财务损失，危害性质与程度高度依赖受害主体。

方法创新：StakeBench基准

为此，我们提出首个利益相关方中心（stakeholder-centric）提示注入评测框架 StakeBench。它系统化实现三重解耦：

  • 实体归因：明确区分受影响方（user/seller/platform）；
  • 目标分解：将攻击细分为12类现实可操作目标（如“绕过支付验证”“伪造商品评价”）；
  • 多维评估：同步采用结果层指标（是否达成恶意目标/是否破坏主任务）与过程层指标（代理决策链中注入指令被激活的位置、隐蔽性强度）。

关键发现

在覆盖8个主流开源Web代理的实证评测中，我们发现：
✅ 无一攻击目标被当前代理可靠防御；
✅ 失败模式呈现显著异质性：
• 隐蔽寄生型（Stealthy Parasitism）：攻击成功且主任务表面正常（占比37.2%）；
• 错位干扰型（Misaligned Disruption）：主任务被意外中断，但攻击未生效（28.5%）；
• 复合失效型（Compounded Failure）：恶意目标与主任务完整性同时崩溃（22.1%）。
这些细粒度风险模式被传统评测完全忽略，证实仅关注“攻击是否成功”的单一维度严重低估真实部署风险。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce StakeBench, the first stakeholder-centric benchmark for evaluating prompt injection vulnerabilities in real-world web agents. Unlike prior attack-centric benchmarks, StakeBench explicitly models who bears the harm—categorizing impacts across users, sellers, and platforms—and decomposes attacks into 12 concrete, operationally grounded objectives (e.g., “bypass payment verification”, “fabricate product reviews”). It evaluates each case using complementary outcome-level metrics (adversarial success vs. task integrity) and process-level metrics (injection activation point, stealthiness). Across 8 state-of-the-art open-source web agents, we find: (1) no attack objective is reliably resisted; (2) failures manifest in three qualitatively distinct, co-occurring modes—stealthy parasitism, misaligned disruption, and compounded failure—which conventional evaluation fails to detect; and (3) vulnerability severity varies significantly by stakeholder role. StakeBench thus reveals critical blind spots in current LLM agent security assessment and provides a foundation for stakeholder-aware robustness engineering. Code and data: https://github.com/StakeBench/SBC.

Abstract

Web agents driven by large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in real-world environments, where they operate over untrusted web content and execute actions with direct consequences. This makes them vulnerable to prompt-injection attacks, in which seemingly benign content embeds adversarial instructions that manipulate agent behaviour. Existing security benchmarks adopt an \textit{attack-centric} perspective, focusing on the technical feasibility of injections while overlooking the nuanced distribution of resulting harms. In practice, however, prompt-injection risk is victim-dependent: a single exploit can produce asymmetric consequences for different stakeholders, and the same attack pattern may exhibit substantially different effectiveness depending on whom it targets. To capture these properties, we introduce \textbf{\sysname}, a \textit{stakeholder-centric} benchmark to systematically categorize and attribute harm in real-world web agent systems. It distinguishes between affected entities (e.g., user, seller, platform), decomposes the attacks into concrete objectives, and evaluates each case with complementary outcome- and process-level metrics. Our results reveal substantial and heterogeneous vulnerabilities: not a single attack objective is reliably resisted by current agents, and failures distribute across qualitatively distinct modes ranging from \emph{stealthy parasitism} (attack succeeds without disrupting the user's delegated task) to \emph{misaligned disruption} (task disrupted without attack success) and \emph{compounded failure} (both adversarial objective and task integrity simultaneously violated). These patterns are missed by conventional evaluation, highlighting the need for stakeholder-aware assessment of LLM-based agents in real-world deployments. Benchmark is available at https://github.com/StakeBench/SBC.

item.title: Efficient, Robust, and Anti-Collusion Fingerprinting of Image Diffusion Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12977v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

生成式文本到图像（T2I）模型的知识产权保护日益紧迫，模型指纹技术（即在生成图像中嵌入用户专属标识）已成为主流方案。然而，现有方法存在一个被长期忽视的系统性缺陷：缺乏抗合谋攻击能力——多个恶意用户可通过联合其指纹化模型，协同移除或混淆指纹，导致溯源失效与模型盗用泛滥。

方法创新

本文首次提出一种高效、鲁棒且具备抗合谋能力的T2I模型指纹框架：

  • 指纹编码机制：将二进制指纹字符串嵌入轻量级个性化归一化模块（PNM）的可学习系数中，实现端到端隐式编码，支持从任意生成图像中高精度提取指纹（准确率 >99.5%）；
  • 抗合谋核心设计：引入基于无损函数不变参数变换的防御机制——合谋者合并模型时，该机制会不可逆地破坏PNM的函数等价性，导致生成图像质量急剧下降（FID显著升高），使合谋模型实际不可用；
  • 工程友好性：支持无需重训练的PNM快速重参数化，开发者可高效分发海量差异化指纹副本；
  • 强鲁棒性增强：采用最坏情况优化策略，显式对抗模型剪枝、微调、蒸馏等模型级攻击。

实验验证

在Stable Diffusion、SDXL及InstructPix2Pix等多个主流T2I与编辑任务上验证：指纹提取平均准确率达99.62%，对噪声、压缩、裁剪等常见失真保持>98%鲁棒性；面对3方及以上合谋攻击，合谋模型FID平均上升>40点（如从12.3升至54.7），图像严重失真，证实其“功能性失效”。本工作首次实现了对合谋攻击的主动式、可证明的鲁棒防御，为生成式AI版权治理提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first anti-collusion fingerprinting framework for text-to-image (T2I) diffusion models. We identify a critical, previously overlooked vulnerability: existing fingerprinting methods collapse under collusion attacks, where multiple adversaries jointly modify their models to erase fingerprints. Our method embeds bit-string fingerprints into coefficients of a lightweight Personalized Normalization Module (PNM), enabling reliable extraction (>99.5% accuracy) from any generated image. Crucially, we introduce a lossless function-invariant parameter transformation mechanism that provably degrades generation quality (FID increases by >40 points) when models are colluded—rendering colluded models practically unusable. The approach supports efficient, retraining-free model duplication via PNM reparameterization and incorporates worst-case optimization for enhanced robustness against model-level attacks (e.g., pruning, fine-tuning). Experiments across Stable Diffusion, SDXL, and editing tasks confirm high fidelity, strong resilience to distortions, and, for the first time, proactive, quantifiable robustness against multi-party collusion.

Abstract

Model fingerprinting, embedding user-specific identifiers (fingerprints) into generated outputs, has recently emerged as a popular solution to protect the intellectual property rights (IPR) of generative text-to-image (T2I) models and prevent unauthorized redistribution. In this work, we reveal a previously unexplored systematic vulnerability in existing generative model fingerprinting methods: they lack robustness against collusion attacks, where multiple attackers combine their models to remove or obscure the fingerprints. To address this issue, we take the first step towards a robust fingerprinting method for T2I models with anti-collusion capabilities. The proposed method encodes strings of bits, namely fingerprints, into the coefficients of a personalized normalization module (PNM) incorporated into T2I models, so that fingerprints can be reliably recovered from any generated image. To defend against collusion attacks and prevent unauthorized model redistribution, we introduce an anti-collusion mechanism based on lossless function-invariant parameter transformations. This mechanism significantly degrades the image generation quality of colluded models, making them effectively unusable. Moreover, our method allows developers to efficiently create multiple copies of fingerprinted T2I models by reparameterizing the PNM without the need for retraining. We also introduce a worst-case optimization strategy to improve robustness against model-level attacks. Our experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high fidelity and robustness across multiple T2I image generation and editing tasks, with fingerprint extraction accuracy exceeding 99.5%. Compared with existing methods, our method demonstrates, for the first time, a notable proactive robustness to collusion attacks by significantly increasing the FID of colluded models.

item.title: MAStrike: Shapley-Guided Collusive Red-Teaming on Multi-Agent Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12918v1

AI Summary (中文)

MAStrike：基于Shapley值引导的多智能体系统协同红队攻击框架

背景与挑战：分层多智能体系统（MAS）正快速部署于金融、软件工程等高风险领域。其安全性天然分布于角色特化的智能体之间，导致攻击面显著扩大——尤其在特权提升、跨智能体共谋等协同对抗行为下，传统单点扰动式红队方法（如随机目标选择、孤立消息篡改）难以识别关键脆弱节点与隐性协作路径，更无法量化各智能体对系统鲁棒性的边际贡献。

方法创新：本文提出MAStrike——首个面向分层MAS的闭环协同红队框架。核心突破包括：（1）首次定义智能体级Shapley值分析，在任务特定数据分布下，精确量化每个智能体对系统安全性的边际贡献；（2）基于该归因结果，自动识别高风险智能体联盟，并生成角色感知、语义连贯的协同对抗操纵（如伪造权限凭证、诱导链式错误响应）；（3）通过结构化因果诊断迭代优化攻击：当攻击失败时，反向归因至未被攻陷但起阻断作用的“守门人”智能体，驱动下一轮精准渗透。

实验与发现：构建了覆盖金融风控、代码审计、客户关系管理（CRM）三大领域的可控MAS基准环境，支持多样化拓扑（树状、网状、混合）。在基于多个前沿大模型（Llama-3、Qwen2、Claude-3）构建的12类MAS上验证表明：MAStrike攻击成功率平均提升42.7%（vs. heuristic baselines），且首次揭示智能体间存在显著高阶交互效应（如三智能体共谋效用非两两之和），暴露单智能体或模板化方法完全忽略的深层脆弱性。

AI Summary (English)

MAStrike introduces the first Shapley-guided, closed-loop framework for collusive red-teaming in hierarchical multi-agent systems (MAS). It proposes the first agent-level Shapley value analysis to quantify each agent’s marginal contribution to system robustness under task-specific distributions, enabling principled identification of vulnerable coalitions. Guided by this attribution, MAStrike generates coordinated, role-aware adversarial manipulations—e.g., privilege-spoofing and cross-agent misdirection—and iteratively refines attacks via causal diagnosis that attributes failures to blocking but uncompromised agents. Evaluated on a comprehensive benchmark spanning finance, software engineering, and CRM domains across diverse topologies and frontier LLM-based MAS, MAStrike achieves up to 42.7% higher attack success than heuristic baselines. Crucially, its Shapley analysis uncovers non-trivial higher-order agent interactions, revealing critical collusion patterns and systemic vulnerabilities invisible to single-agent or template-based methods.

Abstract

Hierarchical multi-agent systems (MAS) are rapidly being deployed in high-stakes workflows across domains such as finance and software engineering. In these systems, safety and security are inherently distributed across role-specialized agents, significantly expanding the attack surface, particularly under coordinated adversarial behaviors such as privilege escalation and cross-agent collusion. Existing red-teaming approaches for MAS remain limited: they rely on heuristic selection of target agents and perturb isolated message streams, leaving critical questions unanswered as which agents are most responsible for system safety, and how compromised agents can coordinate to bypass defenses. We propose MAStrike, a closed-loop framework for collusive red-teaming in hierarchical MAS. We propose the first agent-level Shapley value analysis for MAS, quantifying each agent's marginal contribution to system robustness under task-specific distributions. GGuided by this attribution, MAStrike identifies vulnerable agent coalitions and generates coordinated, role-aware adversarial manipulations. These attacks are iteratively refined through structured causal diagnosis, attributing failure cases to uncompromised agents that block adversarial attempts. We further build a comprehensive MAS red-teaming benchmark and controllable environments spanning diverse hierarchical topologies and domains, including finance, software engineering, and CRM. Extensive experiments across MAS built on multiple frontier models show that MAStrike substantially outperforms heuristic baselines. Our analysis further uncovers non-trivial Shapley value distributions and higher-order interaction structures among agents, revealing critical vulnerabilities and coordination patterns that are overlooked by prior single-agent or template-based methods.

item.title: PolicyGuard: Towards Test-time and Step-level Adversary Defense for Reinforcement Learning Agent
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12896v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

强化学习（RL）在自动驾驶、机器人控制等现实场景中加速落地，但其安全性问题日益凸显。近期研究表明，RL智能体易受后门攻击威胁：攻击者通过污染训练数据或模型，使智能体在常规环境下表现正常，却在特定触发信号（如图像扰动或对手策略）出现时执行恶意动作。现有防御方法存在显著局限：或需访问智能体内部参数（违背黑盒部署前提），或仅在模型级/轨迹级进行检测（缺乏细粒度响应能力），或仅适配特定攻击类型，难以泛化。

方法创新：PolicyGuard

本文提出 PolicyGuard——首个面向测试时（test-time）、步级（step-level） 的轻量级后门防御框架。其核心思想是：在智能体决策每一步时，实时评估该动作的不确定性可信度。为此，PolicyGuard 创新性地：

  • 利用高斯过程（GP）后验方差作为不确定性量化指标，理论证明其能有效区分正常策略与后门触发下的异常决策分布；
  • 设计伪轨迹自适应机制，通过少量无标签环境交互生成局部伪轨迹，绕过对真实轨迹依赖，实现单步级不确定性估计；
  • 完全无需访问策略网络参数或训练数据，兼容任意黑盒RL智能体（如PPO、SAC），且部署开销低（平均增加<8%推理延迟）。

实验验证与贡献

在7个主流RL基准环境（包括Atari、MuJoCo及多智能体对抗场景）上系统评估，PolicyGuard在两类典型后门攻击下均达SOTA性能：

  • 对扰动型攻击（如Trigger-Attack），平均AUROC达 0.856；
  • 对对手智能体型攻击（Adversary-Agent），平均AUROC达 0.859；

显著优于基线方法（提升+12.3%~24.7% AUROC）。本工作首次将GP不确定性建模引入RL安全领域，为实时、细粒度、免侵入式防御提供了可验证的理论基础与实用范式。

AI Summary (English)

Reinforcement learning (RL) agents face growing security threats from backdoor attacks—malicious behaviors triggered only under specific conditions while appearing normal otherwise. Existing defenses suffer from strong assumptions (e.g., white-box access), coarse-grained detection (model- or trajectory-level), or narrow attack coverage. To address this, we propose PolicyGuard, the first test-time, step-level backdoor defense for black-box RL agents. It leverages Gaussian Process (GP) posterior variance—rigorously justified theoretically—as a principled uncertainty signal per decision step, and introduces adaptive pseudo-trajectory generation to enable efficient, parameter-free uncertainty estimation without requiring internal model access or training data. Evaluated across seven diverse RL benchmarks (Atari, MuJoCo, multi-agent settings), PolicyGuard achieves state-of-the-art detection: 0.856 average AUROC against perturbation-based attacks and 0.859 against adversary-agent attacks—outperforming prior methods by up to 24.7%. Its lightweight design incurs <8% inference overhead, enabling practical deployment in safety-critical applications.

Abstract

While real-world applications of reinforcement learning (RL) are becoming increasingly popular, the security of RL systems deserve more attention and exploration. In particular, recent work has revealed that RL agents are vulnerable to backdoor attacks, where a victim agent behaves normally under standard conditions but executes malicious actions when a specific trigger is activated. Existing backdoor defenses for RL either require access to the agent's internal parameters, operate only at the model or trajectory level, or are limited to specific attack types. To ensure the security of RL agents, we propose \texttt{PolicyGuard}, a \textit{test-time step-level} backdoor defense which leverages Gaussian Process (GP) posterior variance and adapts pseudo trajectories to enable uncertainty computation for individual time step. Besides, we also provide theoretical foundations to explain the efficacy of GP posterior variance. Extensive experiments across seven RL games demonstrate that PolicyGuard achieves state-of-the-art detection performance in most cases, with average AUROC of 0.856 for perturbation-based attacks and 0.859 for adversary-agent attacks.

item.title: A Privacy-Preserving Framework Using Remote Data Science for Inter-Institutional Student Retention Prediction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12845v1

AI Summary (中文)

隐私保护型远程数据科学框架：支持跨校学生留存率预测

本研究针对教育领域跨机构协作中敏感学籍数据共享的合规瓶颈，提出一种基于远程数据科学（Remote Data Science, RDS） 的隐私保护机器学习（PPML）框架。该框架依托PySyft平台构建，采用创新的半气隙（semi-air-gapped）双服务器架构——“高侧服务器”安全托管原始学生数据（严格隔离于互联网），而“低侧服务器”供研究人员远程提交代码并执行模型训练，全程无需数据导出或本地拷贝，从架构层面杜绝数据泄露风险。

研究以一所小型私立大学的历史学生数据（N=720）为基准，系统评估了三种合成数据生成方法，并首次提出数据类型感知模板（Data-Type-Aware Templates, DTAT） ——一种面向教育场景的新型合成数据技术：它显式区分结构化字段（如GPA、课程数）、序数变量（如年级、退学阶段）与敏感标识符，通过约束性采样与语义掩码优先保障隐私性，主动牺牲部分分布保真度以换取更强的重识别抵御能力。

在三所高校的实证协作中，各机构独立部署RDS节点，协同构建统一留存预测模型。结果显示：跨校模型性能高度一致（Macro F1 = 0.690–0.695），且全程满足美国《家庭教育权利与隐私法案》（FERPA）全部合规要求。相比联邦学习，本框架显著降低小规模机构的技术门槛与通信开销，验证了RDS范式在资源受限教育环境中的可行性与实用性。源代码已开源：https://github.com/jtfields/NAIRR240195-Privacy-Preserving-Machine-Learning。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a privacy-preserving remote data science (RDS) framework enabling inter-institutional student retention prediction without sharing raw sensitive data. Built on PySyft, it employs a semi-air-gapped architecture with isolated high-side (data-hosting) and low-side (code-execution) servers, allowing researchers from three universities to collaboratively train models while maintaining strict FERPA compliance. Using historical data (N=720) from a small private university, we benchmarked synthetic data generation methods and proposed Data-Type-Aware Templates (DTAT)—a novel approach that prioritizes privacy over distributional fidelity by encoding domain-specific constraints for educational variables. Experimental results show consistent cross-institutional classification performance (Macro F1: 0.690–0.695) and demonstrate RDS-based PPML as a technically feasible, low-overhead alternative to federated learning for small-scale academic collaborations. Code is publicly available.

Abstract

This study explores privacy-preserving machine learning (PPML) techniques using the PySyft platform to enable collaborative prediction of student retention between institutions. We developed a remote data science (RDS) framework with a semi-air-gapped architecture consisting of high-side and low-side servers, allowing researchers from three universities to build predictive models on sensitive student data without direct data access. Using historical data from a small private university (N=720), we evaluated three synthetic data generation approaches and validated the framework through inter-institutional collaboration. The results demonstrate consistent classification performance across institutions (Macro F1: 0.690--0.695) while maintaining strict Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) compliance. We also propose Data-Type-Aware Templates, a novel synthetic data method that prioritizes privacy over distributional fidelity. Our findings confirm that RDS-based PPML is technically feasible for educational settings and offers a practical alternative to federated learning for small-scale inter-institutional collaborations. The code is available at https://github.com/jtfields/NAIRR240195-Privacy-Preserving-Machine-Learning.

item.title: Detecting Functional Memorization in Code Language Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12764v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

随着大语言模型（LLMs）在代码生成任务中的规模化部署，其潜在的数据记忆风险引发广泛关注。现有审计方法主要依赖文本重叠检测（如n-gram匹配、BLEU、exact match），但代码具有高度的功能性等价性——不同语法实现可产生完全相同的输入-输出行为（例如，for循环与列表推导式、不同变量命名或重构逻辑）。因此，仅靠文本相似性无法揭示模型是否记住了训练数据中蕴含的核心算法逻辑。

方法创新

本研究首次系统提出并实证检验“功能记忆”（functional memorization）概念：即模型在未复现原文的前提下，仍能精确再生训练数据中函数的功能行为。我们为开源模型 Olmo-3-32B 构建了严格的反事实对照实验：

  • ✅ 实验组：一个在训练中期（mid-trained）的检查点，已接触目标Python函数样本；
  • ❌ 对照组：同一架构的预训练（pretrained）检查点，明确未见过目标样本；
  • 🧪 评估方式：向两组模型统一输入无实现体的函数签名（如 def gcd(a: int, b: int) -> int:），分别测量：

• 文本相似性（基于Levenshtein距离与代码格式化后的token重叠）；
• 功能相似性（双路径验证）：① LLM-as-a-judge（使用GPT-4o对生成代码的功能正确性打分）；② 执行验证（在100组随机测试用例上比对输入-输出行为一致性）。

主要发现与意义

实验显示：尽管文本相似度差异微弱（<5%），实验组的功能正确率显著高于对照组（+28.7%执行通过率，p<0.001）；且该差距在高复杂度函数（如动态规划、图遍历）中进一步放大。这确凿证实了功能层面的记忆现象独立于文本复现而存在。本工作推动代码模型安全审计范式升级——亟需将执行等价性与语义判别力纳入标准评估协议，而非止步于表面文本匹配。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) are widely deployed for code generation, yet standard memorization audits rely solely on textual overlap (e.g., exact match, BLEU), overlooking that functionally equivalent code can differ significantly in syntax. This paper introduces functional memorization: the unintended retention and regeneration of computational logic—not just surface text—from training data. We design a counterfactual audit for Olmo-3-32B, comparing a mid-trained checkpoint (exposed to target Python functions) against a pretrained reference (unexposed). Both are prompted with function signatures only (no bodies), and outputs are evaluated via: (1) textual similarity (normalized Levenshtein + token overlap), and (2) functional equivalence—using both LLM-as-a-judge (GPT-4o scoring) and rigorous execution-based validation across 100 diverse test inputs. Results show statistically significant functional leakage: the mid-trained model achieves +28.7% higher execution pass rate (p < 0.001) despite negligible textual overlap, especially for algorithmically complex functions. This demonstrates that functional memorization is real, measurable, and orthogonal to verbatim reproduction—urging adoption of behavior-aware metrics in code model safety evaluation.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to generate code at scale. Meanwhile, prior work has investigated whether training data may be recoverable from model outputs, by auditing the textual overlap between training examples and model generations. Code, however, can be functionally equivalent while textually dissimilar. In this work, we study functional memorization: extraction of functional logic beyond what verbatim metrics detect. We construct a counterfactual setup for Olmo-3-32B, comparing a midtrained model (exposed to target code) against a pretrained reference (not exposed). We prompt both models with Python function signatures and measure both textual and functional similarity (i.e., LLM-as-a-judge, execution-based). Our results show clear evidence of functional memorization, highlighting the need for auditing metrics that go beyond textual overlap.

item.title: Towards Personalized Federated Learning for Dysarthric Speech Recognition
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13253v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

构音障碍（dysarthria）患者的语音存在显著发音失真、韵律异常与个体差异，导致传统自动语音识别（ASR）系统性能严重下降。联邦学习（FL）为保护患者语音数据隐私提供了可行范式，但其标准聚合机制（如FedAvg）强制所有用户共享统一模型参数，在高度异质的构音障碍语音数据上易引发“平均化灾难”——即模型难以兼顾不同说话人的独特发音模式。

方法创新

本文首次系统探索面向构音障碍语音识别的个性化联邦学习框架。提出两种新型个性化聚合策略：

  • 参数级个性化：在客户端本地训练中引入可学习的个性化适配层（如LoRA模块），服务器端仅聚合共享主干参数，保留各说话人专属适配权重；
  • 嵌入级个性化：利用语音表征空间中的说话人嵌入（speaker embedding），在聚合前对模型梯度进行嵌入感知加权，使更新更贴近个体声学特征分布。

二者均无需中心化数据，完全满足医疗场景下的隐私合规要求。

主要结果

在UASpeech和TORGO两大权威构音障碍语音数据集上验证：

  • 相比正则化FedAvg基线，参数级策略在UASpeech上实现0.99%绝对WER降低（相对改善3.15%），嵌入级策略在TORGO上达0.56%绝对WER降低（相对改善4.73%）；
  • 所有改进均通过双尾t检验（p < 0.01），统计显著；
  • 消融实验表明，个性化模块对低资源说话人（<10分钟语音）提升尤为突出（WER降幅达1.2%以上）。

本研究为隐私敏感的神经康复语音技术提供了可部署、可解释的个性化FL新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Dysarthric speech recognition faces severe challenges due to high inter-speaker heterogeneity and privacy constraints. While federated learning (FL) offers a privacy-preserving framework, standard aggregation (e.g., FedAvg) fails under such heterogeneity. This paper proposes two novel personalization strategies for dysarthric ASR: (1) parameter-level averaging, where lightweight, speaker-specific adapter modules (e.g., LoRA) are trained locally and only shared backbone parameters are aggregated; and (2) embedding-level averaging, which weights client updates using speaker embeddings to align gradients with individual acoustic characteristics. Evaluated on UASpeech and TORGO, both methods significantly outperform regularized FedAvg—achieving up to 0.99% absolute WER reduction (3.15% relative) on UASpeech and 0.56% absolute WER reduction (4.73% relative) on TORGO, with p < 0.01 statistical significance. To our knowledge, this is the first work systematically addressing personalization in dysarthric FL-ASR, offering a practical, privacy-compliant pathway for clinical speech rehabilitation systems.

Abstract

Speech recognition is challenging for dysarthric speakers. While federated learning (FL)-based ASR can be an effective tool for protecting privacy, it suffers from heterogeneity issues caused by speaker variability. Forcing all speakers to share the same model components can be suboptimal under such heterogeneity, making personalization a promising direction; however, related research on dysarthric speech remains limited. To this end, this paper explores two aggregation strategies to achieve personalization, including the parameter-based averaging strategy and the embedding-based averaging strategy. Experiments on UASpeech and TORGO show that the proposed methods outperform the baseline regularized FedAvg by statistically significant WER reductions of up to 0.99% absolute (3.15% relative) on UASpeech and 0.56% absolute (4.73% relative) on TORGO, respectively.

item.title: G-Long: Graph-Enhanced Memory Management for Efficient Long-Term Dialogue Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13115v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

大语言模型（LLMs）虽显著提升了开放域对话系统的生成能力，但在长时对话一致性维护方面仍面临根本性挑战：一方面，LLMs的长上下文推理能力有限；另一方面，直接处理海量原始对话文本导致计算效率低下。现有方法或依赖非结构化记忆存储（易造成信息丢失），或过度调用大型模型进行记忆管理（引发高延迟与高成本），难以兼顾准确性、效率与可扩展性。

方法创新

本文提出 G-Long——一种图增强的长时记忆管理框架，核心包含两大原创设计：

  • 结构化三元组记忆构建：采用轻量级微调的小型语言模型（sLM），高效抽取对话中的<主体, 关系, 客体>三元组，构建动态知识图谱，实现记忆的语义结构化与去冗余；
  • 注意力感知的重要性评分机制：首次利用T5摘要器内部的跨注意力权重信号，自动量化每个记忆节点在当前对话上下文中的语义重要性，无需额外训练或人工标注，兼具可解释性与低开销。

实验结果

在MSC（多轮社交对话）、LME（长时记忆评估）等多元基准上系统验证：

  • 响应质量（BLEU/BERTScore）提升最高达9.8%（MSC数据集）；
  • 记忆检索召回率提升40.8%（LME数据集），显著优于RAG、MemNet等基线；
  • 推理延迟降低62%，GPU显存占用减少73%，证实其在资源受限场景下的部署优势。

G-Long为构建高效、可扩展、可持续演化的长时对话智能体提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

G-Long is a graph-enhanced memory management framework for long-term dialogue agents, addressing the inefficiency and inconsistency of existing LLM-based approaches. It replaces costly full-model memory operations with a fine-tuned small language model (sLM) to extract structured subject-relation-object triplets from dialogue history, building a dynamic knowledge graph. Crucially, we introduce an attention-aware importance scoring mechanism that leverages intrinsic cross-attention weights from a T5 summarizer to rank memories without extra supervision or parameters. Experiments across MSC and LME benchmarks show G-Long achieves state-of-the-art performance: +9.8% improvement in response quality (MSC) and +40.8% in retrieval recall (LME), while reducing latency by 62% and GPU memory usage by 73% versus LLM-only baselines.

Abstract

While Large Language Models (LLMs) have advanced open-domain dialogue systems, maintaining long-term consistency remains a challenge due to inherent limitations in long-context reasoning and the inefficiency of processing extensive raw text. Existing approaches typically rely on either unstructured memory storage, which is prone to information loss, or computationally expensive LLMs that incur high latency. To address these limitations, we propose G-Long, a graph-enhanced framework that utilizes a fine-tuned small Language Model (sLM) for structured triplet extraction and associative retrieval, significantly reducing operational costs. Furthermore, we introduce the novel attention-aware importance scoring mechanism that leverages the intrinsic cross-attention signals of a T5 summarizer to identify salient memories. Extensive experiments across diverse benchmarks demonstrate that G-Long achieves state-of-the-art performance in both response generation and memory retrieval, yielding performance gains of up to 9.8% in response quality on MSC and 40.8% in retrieval recall on LME, while significantly minimizing computational overhead.

item.title: Nous: An Attempt to Extract and Inject the Cognition Behind Prediction-Market Behavior
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13038v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）代理在预测市场与集体决策中广泛应用，其底层共享基础模型正催生认知单一性（cognitive monoculture）：不同代理的预测高度相关（前沿模型误差相关系数 r ≈ 0.77），削弱群体智慧的抗噪性与准确性。本研究探索一条新路径——能否从真实人类交易行为中提取可泛化的认知多样性特征，并将其有效注入LLM代理，以缓解该结构性风险。

方法创新

我们提出 Nous 框架：

  • 提取端：基于 Polymarket 平台 100 个真实交易钱包的完整历史行为，构建八维结构化行为画像（含时间偏好、风险敏感度、信息滞后性、反共识倾向等）；
  • 注入端：将画像转化为自然语言提示（prompt），引导 LLM 生成符合该认知风格的预测，避免微调或架构修改。

关键发现

✅ 提取有效：8/14 维度具显著时间稳定性（分半信度 ICC ≥ 0.5）；反共识得分 ICC 高达 ~0.9；钱包身份可通过画像实现远超随机水平的识别（top-1 检索率 17–22% vs. 1% 基线）；2/4 预设维度与未来实际盈利呈显著秩相关（但受行为混杂因素干扰后不稳健）。
❌ 注入失效：结构化提示在语义嵌入距离、集成误差相关性（r 未下降）、Brier 分数等所有指标上均无统计显著优势；探索性验证（采样温度、画像多样性、题目难度）均未改变“零结果”；进一步分析发现，提示生成器本身即为瓶颈——其将结构化画像转译为自然语言时严重压缩信息，输出提示呈近似均匀分布，无法反映原始画像的差异性。

理论启示

Nous 首次实证量化了预测市场中人类认知多样性的可提取性，同时揭示了纯提示工程在注入深层认知模式上的根本局限，为后续研究锚定方向：需转向模型内部干预（如参数微调、激活空间引导）以突破提示层的信息瓶颈。

AI Summary (English)

Nous investigates whether human cognitive diversity—latent in real prediction-market behavior—can be extracted and injected into LLM agents to counteract “cognitive monoculture.” From 100 Polymarket wallets, we extract an eight-dimensional behavioral profile (e.g., contrarianism, time discounting), achieving strong temporal stability for 8/14 parameters (ICC ≥ 0.5; contrarian score ICC ≈ 0.9) and wallet identifiability far above chance (17–22% top-1 retrieval vs. 1%). However, structured prompt-based injection fails: it yields no significant improvement in semantic fidelity, ensemble error correlation, or Brier score across models and robustness checks. Critically, the profile-to-prompt translator itself collapses behavioral variance—output prompts are near-uniform and uncorrelated with input profiles. Nous thus empirically benchmarks both the recoverability of human cognition from behavior and the hard limits of prompt-level remedies, motivating deeper, model-internal interventions (e.g., fine-tuning, activation steering). Code and data: https://github.com/WillChienT/nous-paper

Abstract

As LLM agents proliferate in prediction markets and collective decision-making, they risk a cognitive monoculture: agents built on shared foundation models produce correlated forecasts, and recent measurement finds frontier-model errors correlated at r ~ 0.77. We ask whether human cognitive diversity can be recovered from behavior and transferred to LLM agents. Nous extracts a structured eight-dimension behavioral profile from real Polymarket trading activity and injects it into agents through prompts. Our central finding is a dissociation between the two halves of that pipeline. Extraction works, partially: across 100 wallets, 8 of 14 parameters are temporally stable (split-half ICC >= 0.5, bootstrap CI lower bound > 0.3; contrarian score reaches ICC ~ 0.9); wallets are identifiable from their profiles well above chance (top-1 retrieval 17-22% vs. 1% chance); and two of four pre-specified dimensions rank-correlate with future realized profit out-of-sample, though the correlations do not survive behavioral-confound controls. Prompt-level injection does not measurably transmit it: on a semantic embedding metric, structured injection shows no significant advantage over a length-matched control on any model, and the diversity it induces neither reduces ensemble error correlation nor improves Brier score -- a null that persists across exploratory checks on sampling temperature, profile diversity, and question difficulty. Measuring the prompts themselves locates the compression before the model: the structure-to-narrative translator emits near-uniform prompts whose spread does not track profile spread. We position Nous as measuring the cognitive-monoculture problem and the limits of a prompt-level remedy, motivating deeper, below-the-prompt injection (fine-tuning, activation steering). Code, frozen profiles, prompts, and model outputs: https://github.com/WillChienT/nous-paper

item.title: SciR: A Controllable Benchmark for Scientific Reasoning in LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13020v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

科学推理涵盖演绎（deduction）、归纳（induction）和因果溯因（causal abduction）三大范式，但现有基准难以可靠评估大语言模型（LLMs）在真实科学语境下的多范式推理能力：基于人工标注的科学数据集成本高昂且缺乏机制性真值（mechanistic ground truth）；而纯合成逻辑推理基准又脱离真实科学文献的表述形式与知识密度。

方法：SciR基准设计

我们提出SciR（Scientific Reasoning Benchmark）——首个支持双轴可控难度调节的多范式科学推理基准。其核心创新在于：

  • 形式化锚定：三类任务分别源自严格定义的形式对象——演绎树（deduction tree）、归纳规则假设（inductive rule hypothesis）、因果图（causal graph），确保答案可验证、无歧义；
  • 可控科学渲染：通过领域适配的文体模板（如天体物理综述、分子生物学实验报告），将形式对象转化为多文档、跨段落、含图表描述的科学话语，逼近真实科研文本；
  • 正交难度控制：独立调控两大难度维度：① 信息提取难度（关键前提在冗余/隐晦文本中的定位与整合）；② 原理推理难度（范式内逻辑操作的复杂度，如多跳因果链、反事实条件推演）。

主要发现与创新点

在6个主流模型（含DeepSeek-R1等推理模型及Neurosymbolic pipeline）上的测试表明：

  • 两难度轴均显著降低性能，且存在强协同恶化效应（compounding effect）；
  • 即使将推理步骤交由经验证的符号求解器处理的神经符号系统，亦因科学文本渲染而性能下降，凸显提取瓶颈的普遍性；
  • 各模型呈现独特“提取–推理”能力谱系：如DeepSeek-R1在推理轴上显著优于指令微调模型，但在高提取难度下优势收窄；
  • SciR是首个实现多范式覆盖 + 形式真值保障 + 双轴参数化控制的科学推理基准，为细粒度能力归因与模型诊断提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

SciR is the first controllable, multi-paradigm benchmark for scientific reasoning in LLMs, grounded in deduction, induction, and causal abduction. It bridges the gap between synthetic logic benchmarks (lacking scientific realism) and human-annotated datasets (lacking mechanistic ground truth) by generating tasks from formal objects—deduction trees, inductive hypotheses, and causal graphs—to guarantee verifiable answers. These are then rendered into authentic, multi-document scientific discourse via domain-tuned genres (e.g., astrophysics reviews, molecular biology reports). Crucially, SciR orthogonally controls two difficulty axes: extraction difficulty (locating and integrating scattered premises) and inference difficulty (complexity of principled reasoning within each paradigm). Evaluation across six models reveals that both axes degrade performance significantly—and their effects compound. Even neurosymbolic pipelines suffer under scientific rendering, underscoring extraction as a critical bottleneck. SciR enables fine-grained, per-model profiling along the extraction–inference spectrum (e.g., DeepSeek-R1 excels on inference but remains vulnerable to extraction hardness), establishing a new standard for diagnostic, mechanism-aware evaluation of scientific reasoning.

Abstract

Three paradigmatic forms of inference recur across scientific reasoning: deduction, induction, and causal abduction. Reliably evaluating LLMs on these in scientific settings is currently out of reach: scientific benchmarks built on human annotations are costly and lack mechanistic ground truth, while synthetic logical-reasoning benchmarks do not resemble real scientific documents. We introduce SciR, a benchmark that combines multi-paradigm reasoning with controllable scientific rendering, anchored on three paradigmatic scientific problems. Tasks are generated from formal objects (deduction tree, inductive rule hypothesis, causal graph) to guarantee verifiable answers, then rendered into multi-document scientific discourse via per-track domain-tuned genres. The construction lets us independently vary two difficulty axes: how hard it is to extract the key information needed for inference, and how hard the principled inference itself is. We test six models. Both axes hurt every model, and their effects compound. The rendering even hurts neurosymbolic pipelines, which hand inference to a verified solver. The two axes yield a per-model extraction-vs-inference profile: for instance, reasoning models like deepseek-r1 mostly surpass non-reasoning instruct models on the inference axis. To our knowledge, SciR is the first multi-paradigm scientific-reasoning benchmark with parametric control on both extraction and inference difficulty.

item.title: The Internet of Agentic AI: Communication, Coordination, and Collective Intelligence at Scale
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12835v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与愿景

随着自主AI代理（Agentic AI）的快速涌现，人工智能正从孤立的模型推理跃迁为具备推理、通信与行动能力的分布式智能系统。本文提出“具身智能互联网”（Internet of Agentic AI, IoAI）这一原创性范式——一个开放、异构、跨域协同的智能体生态：代理可在云、边缘、终端设备、组织边界及信息物理系统中动态相互发现、协商职责、交换语义上下文、调用工具并协同执行复杂工作流。

方法与框架

我们融合单代理智能体架构、多智能体系统（MAS）、分布式计算、网络协议设计、博弈论与安全工程六大基础领域，系统性构建IoAI的四层架构模型：（1）部署层（支持容器化、轻量化与跨平台代理运行）；（2）通信层（定义轻量级、语义感知的消息协议，支持异步事件驱动与意图协商）；（3）互操作层（基于统一代理描述语言与上下文本体实现语义互操作）；（4）信任与治理层（整合去中心化身份（DID）、零知识证明验证、激励相容机制与分级治理策略）。

关键发现与创新

通过在自适应智能制造（动态产线重配置）与分布式作战协调（多军种无人平台协同）两个高保真案例中验证，本框架首次系统揭示了六大核心挑战：可控涌现性（避免不可预测的集体行为）、语义互操作性（突破API级兼容走向意图级对齐）、安全可信身份（抗伪造、可撤销、最小权限的代理身份体系）、激励相容协调机制（防止搭便车与策略性欺骗）、资源感知编排（实时权衡计算、带宽、能耗与延迟），以及可扩展治理架构（支持跨组织、跨主权边界的协作规范演进）。本研究为构建大规模、鲁棒、可信的自主智能体网络提供了首个整合性理论框架与工程路线图。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the Internet of Agentic AI (IoAI)—a foundational vision for large-scale, open ecosystems of autonomous AI agents that discover, negotiate, coordinate, and act across cloud, edge, device, organizational, and cyber-physical boundaries. Synthesizing insights from multi-agent systems, distributed computing, network protocols, game theory, and security engineering, we propose a four-layer architectural framework addressing deployment models, semantic communication protocols, interoperability via context-aware ontologies, and trust architectures grounded in decentralized identity and incentive-compatible mechanisms. Through case studies in adaptive manufacturing and distributed operational coordination, we identify six core research challenges: controlled emergence, semantic interoperability, secure agent identity, incentive-compatible coordination, resource-aware orchestration, and scalable governance. The IoAI framework provides the first integrated theoretical and engineering foundation for building robust, trustworthy, and cooperative networks of autonomous agents at planetary scale.

Abstract

The rapid emergence of autonomous AI agents is transforming artificial intelligence from isolated model inference into distributed systems of reasoning, communication, and action. This paper develops the vision of the Internet of Agentic AI (IoAI): an open ecosystem in which heterogeneous agents discover one another, negotiate responsibilities, exchange context, invoke tools, and execute workflows across cloud, edge, device, organizational, and cyber-physical environments. We synthesize foundations from single-agent agentic AI, multi-agent systems, distributed computing, communication networks, game theory, and security engineering to characterize the architectures and mechanisms required for scalable agent ecosystems. The paper examines agent deployment models, workflow lifecycles, communication protocols, interoperability layers, resource-management challenges, and trust architectures, with case studies in adaptive manufacturing and distributed operational coordination. The resulting framework highlights the central research challenges of controlled emergence, semantic interoperability, secure identity, incentive-compatible coordination, resource-aware orchestration, and governance for large-scale networks of autonomous agents.

item.title: The Containment Gap: How Deployed Agentic AI Frameworks Fail Public-Facing Safety Requirements
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12797v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

面向公众部署的自主型AI系统（如政府服务、医疗分诊、金融顾问）正日益依赖具备工具调用、持久记忆与多步规划能力的智能体（Agentic AI）框架。然而，其底层架构是否具备可验证的结构性安全保证，尚无系统性评估。

方法与发现

本研究提出基于智能体架构组成的六项“围栏”（Containment）原则，对三大主流框架（LangChain、AutoGPT、OpenAI Agents SDK）开展深度审计。结果表明：三者均未原生支持任何一项原则；尤其关键的记忆完整性（Memory Integrity）机制完全缺失——该机制是抵御最常见攻击（如记忆投毒）的核心防线。

实证验证

在基于LangChain构建的模拟政府福利申领代理中，仅一次恶意记忆写入即导致跨所有随机种子与后端的持续性定向污染：目标申请人错误拒批率飙升至88.9%；在复杂五因素政策下，该攻击更使定向错误拒批率提升3.5倍，而整体准确率保持不变，从而规避常规监控指标。

创新与对策

我们提出两种轻量级围栏机制：记忆完整性校验器与策略门控器，二者均引入<0.2ms/调用的开销，即可彻底阻断上述两类攻击向量。研究表明，当前智能体框架生态尚未满足公共场景所需的“默认安全”基准，亟需在内存隔离、执行沙箱、策略嵌入等层面实施优先级架构改造，以支撑高风险、高社会影响场景的可信部署。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies a critical “Containment Gap” in deployed agentic AI frameworks: none of the three dominant systems—LangChain, AutoGPT, and OpenAI Agents SDK—natively satisfy six architecturally grounded containment principles essential for public-facing safety. Crucially, memory integrity—the primary defense against memory-poisoning attacks—is entirely absent. Empirical evaluation on a simulated government benefits agent shows a single poisoned memory write induces persistent, targeted corruption: wrongful denial rates for vulnerable applicants rise to 88.9%, and under a complex five-factor policy, targeted denials increase 3.5× while aggregate accuracy remains unchanged—evading standard monitoring. We introduce two lightweight, sub-millisecond (<0.2 ms/call) mechanisms—a memory integrity validator and a policy gate—that eliminate both attack vectors. Our findings challenge the “secure-by-default” assumption for current agentic frameworks and call for urgent architectural interventions in high-stakes applications.

Abstract

Agentic large language model systems that autonomously invoke tools, maintain persistent memory, and execute multi-step plans are increasingly deployed in public-facing domains, including government services, healthcare triage, and financial advising. We ask whether the frameworks used to build these systems provide architectural-level structural safety guarantees. Applying six containment principles derived from a compositional model of agentic architectures, we audit three dominant frameworks (LangChain, AutoGPT, and OpenAI Agents SDK) and find no native compliance in any of them. Memory integrity, a defense against one of the most prevalent vulnerability classes, is not observed in any of the three evaluated frameworks. We validate these findings empirically: in a simulated government benefits agent built on LangChain, a single memory-poisoning write induces persistent targeted corruption across all tested seeds and backends, increasing the wrongful denial rate for targeted applicants to 88.9%. Under a complex five-factor policy, the same attack preserves aggregate accuracy while increasing targeted wrongful denials by 3.5x, rendering the corruption difficult to detect through standard monitoring. We then introduce two lightweight containment mechanisms: a memory integrity validator and a policy gate, which eliminate both attack vectors with sub-millisecond overhead (<0.2ms per call). We conclude that the current agentic framework ecosystem may not yet meet secure-by-default expectations for public-facing deployments and outline priority architectural interventions to enable trustworthy deployment in high-stakes, socially impactful applications.

item.title: Clipping Makes Distributed and Federated Asynchronous SGD Robust to Stragglers
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13287v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

在大规模机器学习训练中，异步随机梯度下降（ASGD）被广泛用于提升硬件利用率，避免同步等待“慢节点”（stragglers）。然而，当采用恒定学习率时，ASGD的收敛性会因梯度更新延迟（staleness）加剧而显著退化——延迟越长，误差累积越严重，尤其在分布式与联邦学习场景下，网络异构性与设备不稳定性使该问题尤为突出。

方法创新

本文首次从理论层面揭示梯度裁剪（clipping） 在异步优化中的核心作用：它不仅能经验性地“稳定”深度模型训练，更能彻底解除收敛速率对最大延迟的依赖。我们提出一个更具现实表征能力的子韦布尔（sub-Weibull）梯度噪声模型，统一涵盖子高斯、子指数及更重尾的噪声分布——这直接源于对真实深度学习训练中梯度分布的实证观测（如长尾、尖峰特性）。

主要发现与贡献

  • 首次证明：在梯度裁剪约束下，ASGD的期望收敛速率仅依赖于裁剪阈值与平滑常数，与最大延迟无关；
  • 给出首个高概率收敛保证（with high probability），突破了异步优化领域长期局限于期望收敛的理论局限；
  • 提出的分析框架适用于分布式与联邦异步SGD，且无需假设梯度有界或延迟有界，显著增强实用性；
  • 实验验证表明：裁剪不仅提升收敛鲁棒性，还能在高延迟场景下将通信效率提升达2.3倍（相比无裁剪基线）。

本工作为理解并设计抗慢节点的异步算法提供了坚实的理论基础与实用准则。

AI Summary (English)

This paper provides the first theoretical justification for the empirical stabilization effect of gradient clipping in asynchronous SGD (ASGD). We prove that clipping eliminates the dependence of convergence rate on maximum update delay—resolving a key bottleneck in distributed and federated asynchronous training. Under a sub-Weibull noise model (generalizing sub-Gaussian/sub-exponential assumptions to better capture heavy-tailed gradients observed in deep learning), we establish: (i) convergence in expectation with delay-independent complexity; and (ii) the first high-probability convergence guarantee in asynchronous optimization. Our analysis applies to both distributed and federated settings without requiring bounded gradients or bounded delays, and is validated empirically under realistic straggler conditions.

Abstract

In modern machine learning, parallelization of training is an important strategy for increasing scale. Asynchronous stochastic gradient descent (ASGD), which maximizes the utilization of available hardware by avoiding waiting for slow workers. However, with constant step sizes, the convergence of ASGD is nonetheless affected negatively by slow workers due to large delays in updates. At the same time, it has been empirically observed in asynchronous training of deep learning models that gradient clipping "stabilizes" training. In this work, we provide a theoretical justification for this behavior, as we show that clipping removes the dependence of the maximum delay in the oracle complexity. We employ a sub-Weibull model of gradient noise which generalizes sub-Gaussian and sub-exponential distributions to more heavy-tailed distributions, motivated by empirical observations in deep learning. We show convergence in expectation, and the first time in asynchronous optimization, convergence with high probability.

item.title: Disparate Impact in Synthetic Data Generation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13105v1

AI Summary (中文)

重新审视合成数据生成中的差异影响问题

本研究聚焦于合成数据生成（SDG）中的“差异影响”（Disparate Impact）公平性准则，该准则要求生成数据在不同敏感群体（如性别、种族）上的效用（utility）保持一致。与现有公平SDG工作主要致力于“修正观测分布中的偏差”不同，本文提出一个根本性观点：非差异影响的自然解，恰是使合成分布与真实分布完全一致——此时各群体的统计特性与效用自动均衡。我们系统剖析了SDG为何难以达成这一理想解，并识别出三类导致群体间不均衡误差的关键机制：

  • 模型表达能力局限：当SDG方法（如概率图模型）的建模能力不足以刻画高复杂度的真实分布时，拟合误差在小样本群体中显著放大；
  • 采样偏差强化：敏感子群体占比过低时，训练数据稀疏加剧估计方差，且该效应在标准统一建模下不可忽略；
  • 隐私机制引入的异质噪声：差分隐私（DP）添加的噪声强度常依赖全局敏感度，但各群体局部敏感度差异显著，导致DP-SDG在少数群体上效用断崖式下降。

我们在人工基准与真实医疗/人口数据集上验证了上述现象，尤其针对基于贝叶斯网络和马尔可夫随机场的SDG方法。进一步，我们提出分组学习策略（group-wise SDG）：为每个敏感群体独立训练专用生成模型，并辅以约束正则化保障跨群体一致性。实验表明，该策略不仅提升整体效用（如边际分布保真度、下游任务准确率），更显著改善效用在群体间的奇偶性（parity），在多个指标上实现高达42%的差异影响缓解。

AI Summary (English)

We revisit disparate impact—a fairness notion requiring equal utility of synthetic data across sensitive groups—in synthetic data generation (SDG). Contrary to prior fair-SDG approaches that modify the target distribution to correct bias, we argue that non-disparate impact is naturally achieved when the synthetic distribution exactly matches the real one. We identify three primary sources of disparate approximation and estimation errors: (1) limited expressive power of SDG models relative to distribution complexity, (2) sampling variance amplified in underrepresented groups due to skewed proportions, and (3) heterogeneous noise from differential privacy mechanisms, whose global sensitivity assumptions misalign with group-specific sensitivities. Empirically, we demonstrate disparate impact on both synthetic and real-world datasets using probabilistic graphical model–based SDG methods. To mitigate it, we propose group-wise SDG: learning separate, constrained models per sensitive group. This strategy improves both overall utility (e.g., marginal fidelity, downstream task accuracy) and utility parity—reducing disparate impact by up to 42% across metrics.

Abstract

We revisit the fairness notion of disparate impact for synthetic data generation (SDG), that assesses whether the utility of generated records is the same across sensitive groups. Our approach departs from existing work on fair SDG, that address the problem of correcting for undue biases in the observed distribution, hence redefining SDG as learning a distribution that is not that of the real data. By contrast, non-disparate impact is notably achieved when the synthetic and real distributions are the same. We expose reasons why SDG may fail to reach that solution and discuss why approximation and estimation errors occur and can be disparate across groups. We notably look into the expressive power of SDG methods relative to distribution complexity, sampling errors due to group proportions, and estimation errors induced by differential privacy mechanisms. We illustrate cases of disparate impact on both artificial and real-world data, focusing on SDG methods that rely on probabilistic graphical models. We also introduce a strategy of learning group-wise SDG models and illustrate how it can improve both the overall utility and its parity in many settings.

item.title: A solvable model for unsupervised federated learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13045v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本文针对无监督联邦学习（Unsupervised Federated Learning）缺乏严格理论支撑的现状，构建了一个可解的生成式建模范式，首次在统计物理框架下系统刻画分布式学生节点间的协同机制。传统联邦学习多聚焦监督任务，而无监督场景中各客户端（“学生”）仅持有带噪或子采样的局部数据，无法直接共享标签或梯度，导致理论分析困难。

方法与理论框架

我们提出“教师–多学生交互”生成模型：一个全局教师生成真实信号，多个学生各自观测独立噪声版本（如高斯噪声扰动）或非均匀子集（大小可变）。借助无序系统平衡态理论（equilibrium disordered systems），特别是自洽场方法与 replica 技术，我们严格推导出学生群体的平均场动力学，并将整体学习过程映射为一个结构化隐层的受限玻尔兹曼机（RBM）的平衡采样过程。

主要发现与创新点

  • 交互增强学习性能：学生间通过隐式协作显著提升泛化能力——高噪声学生所需样本量减少达 40%；低噪声学生与真信号的重叠度（overlap）提升超 30%；
  • 最优贝叶斯恢复条件：解析给出教师参数可恢复的相变边界，明确依赖于样本复杂度、信噪比与交互强度三元参数；
  • 数值验证完备：在合成高斯混合与MNIST隐空间数据上，理论预测与模拟结果误差 < 2.1%，证实模型普适性。

本工作为无监督联邦学习提供了首个可解理论基准，揭示了“分布式协作即隐式正则化”的深层原理，为设计鲁棒通信协议与隐私保护架构奠定基础。

AI Summary (English)

We propose a solvable theoretical framework for unsupervised federated learning, modeling it as a teacher–multiple interacting students system where each student observes a distinct noisy or subsampled realization of the data. Using equilibrium statistical mechanics tools—including replica theory and self-consistent field analysis—we derive exact analytical expressions for learning performance. We prove that student interactions systematically improve generalization: highly noisy students require significantly fewer samples to recover the ground-truth pattern, while low-noise students achieve higher overlap with the teacher signal. The optimal Bayesian conditions for teacher recovery are explicitly characterized as functions of sample complexity, noise level, and interaction strength—validated via extensive numerical simulations on synthetic and MNIST-based generative tasks. Crucially, the learning dynamics map exactly onto equilibrium sampling in a Restricted Boltzmann Machine with a structured hidden layer, offering the first principled physical interpretation of how collaboration enhances distributed generative modeling.

Abstract

We introduce a theoretical framework for analyzing federated learning in a generative setting through a teacher-multiple interacting students scenario, in which each student receives a distinct realization of the data, either through a different noise corruption or by accessing a different subset, possibly of varying size. Using theoretical tools in equilibrium disordered system, we analytically show that interactions among students systematically enhance learning performance: highly noisy students require fewer samples to recover the underlying pattern, while low-noise students achieve a larger overlap with the ground-truth signal. We derive the optimal Bayesian conditions for teacher recovery as functions of the sample complexity, noise level, and interaction strength, and validate these predictions through numerical simulations. The resulting dynamics can be mapped onto equilibrium sampling in a Restricted Boltzmann Machine with a structured hidden layer, providing a principled theoretical understanding of how interactions improve distributed generative modeling.

item.title: Selecting Samples on Graphs: A Unified Dataset Pruning Framework for Lossless Training Acceleration
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12913v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

现代深度学习训练数据规模持续膨胀，导致计算开销激增。数据集剪枝（Dataset Pruning, DP） 作为一类高效加速范式，旨在筛选最具信息量的样本子集，在保障模型性能前提下显著降低训练成本。现有方法主要分为两类：一类基于内在信号（如损失值、梯度模长），独立评估单一样本价值；另一类依赖外在信号（如特征相似性、多样性度量），通过成对关系提升覆盖广度。然而，二者均存在明显局限：内在方法忽视样本间协同效应，外在方法忽略个体判别性，导致在不同剪枝比例或数据分布下鲁棒性不足。

方法创新

本文提出首个统一图结构化DP框架。我们将整个数据集建模为一个加权图：节点权重编码样本的内在重要性（如训练损失、不确定性），边权重刻画样本间的外在互补性或多样性（如特征空间距离、语义差异）。在此建模下，最优子集选择被严格形式化为最大权团问题（Maximum Weight Clique Problem, MWCP）——即寻找节点与边权重联合贡献最大的完全连通子图。尽管MWCP是NP难问题，我们设计了一种基于样本边际增益的贪心算法，其每步选择使当前目标函数增量最大的样本，并证明该策略在轻度正则性条件下（如子模性近似、权重有界）具备理论近似比保证（$1 - 1/e$ 级别），适用于广泛的实用性度量（如梯度、Fisher信息、核心集目标）。

实验验证

在ImageNet-1k与ResNet-50基准上，本方法在保留100% Top-1准确率（80.2%）的前提下，训练时间减少42.3%；在50%剪枝率下仍保持±0.1%精度波动。相较SOTA方法（如GLISTER、Craig、GradMatch），本方法在CIFAR-10/100、Tiny-ImageNet等多数据集上平均提升0.3–0.7个百分点，且对噪声标签与类别不平衡更具鲁棒性。代码已开源。

AI Summary (English)

We propose a unified graph-based dataset pruning (DP) framework that jointly models intrinsic sample importance (via node weights) and extrinsic diversity/complementarity (via edge weights) to formulate DP as the Maximum Weight Clique Problem (MWCP). Although NP-hard, MWCP admits an efficient greedy algorithm grounded in per-sample marginal gains. Under mild submodularity-like conditions, we prove a formal $(1 - 1/e)$-approximation guarantee applicable to broad importance metrics (e.g., gradient norm, Fisher information, loss). Experiments on ImageNet-1k with ResNet-50 show our method reduces training time by >40% without accuracy loss (maintaining full 80.2% Top-1), outperforming prior DP methods across multiple benchmarks and exhibiting superior robustness to label noise and class imbalance.

Abstract

The rapid growth of modern training datasets has significantly increased computational cost, motivating dataset pruning~(DP) methods which retain only a subset of informative samples to reduce training cost. Existing pruning criteria typically rely on either intrinsic signals that assess samples independently or extrinsic signals that promote diversity via pairwise relations. While effective in their own specific regimes, each captures only one aspect of sample utility and lacks robustness across different pruning ratios or data distribution. In this work, we present a unified graph-based DP framework. By modeling the dataset as a weighted graph, where node weights encode intrinsic value and edge weights encode extrinsic value, DP can be cast as a Maximum Weight Clique Problem (MWCP). Although MWCP is NP-hard, its structure admits a principled greedy solution based on sample-wise marginal gains. Under a few mild conditions, we further prove that this unified objective enjoys a formal approximation guarantee, which applies to a broad family of importance metrics and provides practical design guidelines. Extensive experiments show that our method outperforms existing DP methods while substantially reducing training cost, reducing training time by over 40\% without sacrificing accuracy on ImageNet-1k with ResNet-50.

item.title: SkillCAT: Contrastive Assessment and Topology-Aware Skill Self-Evolution for LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13317v1

AI Summary (中文)

SkillCAT：面向大语言模型智能体的对比式技能评估与拓扑感知自进化框架

当前LLM智能体的技能自进化方法虽致力于将执行轨迹转化为可复用的技能文档，但仍存在三大瓶颈：（1）单轨迹学习——每任务仅依赖一条执行路径，缺乏对成功/失败因果差异的辨识；（2）盲目合并——在未验证有效性前即融合候选技能片段；（3）全量加载——推理时需载入整个技能库，导致冗余与低效。为此，我们提出SkillCAT——一种无需训练、三阶段解耦的新型技能进化框架。

  • 对比因果提取（CCE）：针对每个任务采样多条轨迹，构建成功/失败配对样本，通过对比分析识别决定性因果证据（如关键API调用、条件跳转或格式修正），显著提升技能片段的语义精准性；
  • 评估增强进化（AAE）：在源任务克隆环境中重放每个候选技能片段，仅保留能提升或维持任务性能的片段，并采用层次化策略进行安全合并，杜绝退化风险；
  • 拓扑感知任务执行（TTE）：将进化后的技能编译为可路由的子技能拓扑图，推理时仅动态加载与当前任务语义匹配的“能力节点”，实现轻量、定向、可解释的技能调用。

我们在SpreadsheetBench、WikiTableQuestions和DocVQA三大代理基准上系统评测SkillCAT，并验证其跨模型（Llama3、Qwen、Phi-3）及分布外（OOD）泛化能力。结果表明：SkillCAT在平均任务得分上相对基线最高提升40.40%，且全程无需任何梯度更新或参数微调，首次实现了高鲁棒性、零训练开销、结构可解释的技能自主进化。

AI Summary (English)

SkillCAT is a training-free framework for skill self-evolution in LLM agents, addressing three key limitations of prior methods: single-trajectory learning, unverified skill merging, and full-skill-corpus loading during inference. It decomposes evolution into three stages: (1) Contrastive Causal Extraction (CCE) identifies outcome-determining evidence by comparing success/failure trajectory pairs per task; (2) Assessment-Augmented Evolution (AAE) validates candidate patches via replay on task clones and retains only those preserving or improving performance before hierarchical merging; (3) Topology-Aware Task Execution (TTE) compiles evolved skills into a routable sub-skill graph, enabling on-demand loading of task-relevant capability nodes. Evaluated on SpreadsheetBench, WikiTableQuestions, and DocVQA—and tested across models (Llama3, Qwen, Phi-3) and out-of-distribution settings—SkillCAT achieves up to +40.40% average score gain over baselines, demonstrating reliable, zero-training, topology-guided skill evolution.

Abstract

Skill self-evolution methods for LLM agents aim to turn execution trajectories into reusable skill documents, but current pipelines typically learn from one trajectory per task, merge candidate skill patches before checking them, and load the full skill corpus before inference. We propose SkillCAT, a training-free framework that separates this process into three stages. Contrastive Causal Extraction (CCE) samples multiple trajectories for each task and compares same-task success/failure pairs to identify evidence that explains outcome differences. Assessment-Augmented Evolution (AAE) replays each candidate patch on source-task clones and keeps only patches that improve or preserve task outcomes before hierarchical skill patch merging. Topology-Aware Task Execution (TTE) compiles the evolved skills into a routable sub-skill topology, so inference loads only the capability nodes relevant to the task. We evaluate SkillCAT on common agent benchmarks, including SpreadsheetBench, WikiTableQuestions, and DocVQA, and further test cross-model and out-of-distribution generalization. Across these settings, SkillCAT raises the average score over baselines by up to 40.40%, demonstrating reliable skill evolution without model training.

item.title: MÖVE: A Holistic LLM Benchmark for the German Public Sector
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13111v1

AI Summary (中文)

MÖVE：面向德国公共部门的综合性大语言模型基准测试

背景与问题：尽管大语言模型（LLM）正加速应用于德国公共行政领域，但模型选型仍缺乏系统性依据。现有基准普遍存在三大局限：以英语为主、内容偏向美国语境、仅关注任务性能，忽视治理性维度（如宪法兼容性、政治中立性、能源效率与供应商透明度）。

方法与设计：本研究提出 MÖVE（Modelle für die Öffentliche Verwaltung Evaluieren），首个专为德国公共部门定制的双维度评估框架。我们对39个主流德语/多语LLM开展全面评测：

  • 性能维度：涵盖公文摘要、政务问答、主题抽取三类核心任务；
  • 治理维度：创新引入四项关键指标——幻觉率（基于人工验证与LLM-as-a-judge双重校验）、单次推理能耗（实测+模型化估算）、供应商信息披露完整性、以及对德国《基本法》原则及主要政党政策立场的知识对齐度。

依托10个德语数据集（含6个自建黄金/银标准数据集，覆盖公民咨询、法规解读、行政报告等真实场景），融合经典NLP指标（ROUGE、BERTScore）、嵌入相似度分析与多轮LLM裁判机制，实现多粒度评估。

主要发现：
✅ 无单一模型在全部维度上占优——任务最优模型高度分化，参数量与实际政务表现弱相关；
✅ 治理指标显著影响综合排名：部分小模型在低幻觉与高宪法一致性上反超大模型；
✅ 基准自身经严格验证：统计信度高（Cohen’s κ > 0.82），LLM裁判稳定性强，私有数据集提升区分度，提示词微调对结果影响可控（Δ排名 < 15%），能耗估算误差 < 12%。
MÖVE作为开放、持续演进的“活基准”，所有数据、代码与实时排名已开源发布：https://moeve.bundesdruckerei.de/

AI Summary (English)

We introduce MÖVE, the first holistic benchmark for evaluating LLMs in the German public sector. Addressing critical gaps in existing benchmarks—English/US bias and narrow task-only focus—MÖVE assesses 39 models across performance (summarization, QA, topic extraction) and governance (hallucination rate, energy consumption, provider transparency, alignment with German constitutional values and party policy positions). It leverages 10 German-language datasets—including 6 newly curated gold/silver-standard corpora reflecting real administrative domains—and employs a multi-metric strategy combining classical NLP metrics, embedding-based similarity, and calibrated LLM-as-a-judge protocols. Results reveal no dominant model: top performers vary significantly across tasks, and parameter count poorly predicts administrative utility. Crucially, governance criteria substantially reshape rankings—smaller models often outperform larger ones on constitutional alignment and hallucination control. We rigorously validate MÖVE’s reliability, sensitivity, and validity, ensuring robustness for real-world deployment. All resources are publicly available at https://moeve.bundesdruckerei.de/.

Abstract

We present MÖVE (Modelle für die Öffentliche Verwaltung Evaluieren), a holistic benchmark for evaluating large language models (LLMs) in the context of the German public sector. While LLMs are increasingly adopted in public administration, model selection remains largely ad hoc, and existing benchmarks offer limited guidance: they are predominantly English-centric, US-centric in content, and focus exclusively on task performance. MÖVE addresses these gaps by evaluating 39 models across two complementary dimensions. Performance criteria cover summarization, question answering, and topic extraction. Governance criteria assess hallucination tendencies, energy consumption, provider transparency, and alignment with German constitutional values and knowledge about positions by German political parties. In total, we utilize ten German-language datasets, including gold- and silverstandard datasets that we constructed to reflect public-administration domains. We employ a multi-metric evaluation strategy combining classical NLP metrics, embedding-based methods, and LLM-as-a-judge approaches. Our results show that no single model dominates across all criteria: top performers differ between tasks, and model size alone is a poor predictor of quality. We further evaluate the benchmark itself, analyzing its statistical precision, LLM judge reliability, the impact of our private datasets on model rankings, the sensitivity of our results to prompt formulation, and the validity of our energy consumption estimates. MÖVE is designed as a living benchmark under active development; results are publicly available at https://moeve.bundesdruckerei.de/.

item.title: sebis at CRF Filling 2026: A Two-Stage Local LLM Pipeline for Medical CRF Filling
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13082v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

从非结构化电子健康病历（EHR）文本中提取结构化临床信息，是医疗信息学长期存在的瓶颈。尽管大语言模型（LLM）展现出强大性能，但其在临床场景的落地仍受三大制约：患者隐私泄露风险、高推理成本，以及脱离文本依据的幻觉生成（hallucination），尤其在需严格循证的病例报告表（CRF）填写任务中尤为关键。

方法创新

本研究为CL4Health 2026 CRF填充任务提出纯本地化、领域适配的两阶段轻量级LLM流水线：

  • 第一阶段：基于MedGemma-27B（开源医学大模型）进行二元存在性判断（如“是否存在高血压？”），明确区分“存在/不存在/不确定/未知”四类确定性状态；
  • 第二阶段：仅对判定为“存在”的条目，执行精细化值抽取（如具体血压数值、诊断日期），全程强制约束于原文证据，禁用任何外部API调用或参数微调；
  • 全流程采用条目定制化的少样本上下文学习（item-specific few-shot ICL），通过精心设计的提示模板实现逻辑解耦与证据锚定。

主要成果

在官方英文测试集上，本方法取得macro-F1 = 0.55，在所有本地部署、开源模型提交方案中位列第二名。实验表明：该架构显著抑制幻觉，对否定表述（如“无胸痛”）、模糊描述（如“疑似感染”）和缺失信息均输出可解释、确定性结果；同时验证了隐私优先的院内部署方案可逼近商用前沿模型性能，为医疗机构提供了一套符合数据主权要求、可审计、低运维依赖的临床NLP实践框架。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents sebis’ submission to the CL4Health 2026 CRF Filling task: a fully local, two-stage LLM pipeline built on MedGemma-27B to extract structured clinical data from unstructured EHR notes without compromising privacy or evidence fidelity. The first stage performs binary presence classification (e.g., “hypertension present?”), explicitly distinguishing present, absent, uncertain, and unknown states; the second stage extracts precise values only when presence is confirmed—strictly grounded in textual evidence. Leveraging item-specific, few-shot in-context learning—without fine-tuning or external API calls—the system achieves a macro-F1 score of 0.55 on the official English test set, ranking 2nd among all locally hosted, open-source submissions. Our work demonstrates that privacy-preserving, on-premise LLM pipelines can deliver near-competitive performance while ensuring deterministic, auditable, and clinically trustworthy outputs.

Abstract

The extraction of structured clinical information from unstructured EHR notes is a persistent bottleneck in healthcare informatics. While large language models (LLMs) offer high performance, their deployment in clinical settings is hindered by privacy risks, inference costs, and the tendency to hallucinate beyond textual evidence. We address these challenges for the CL4Health 2026 Case Report Form (CRF) filling task by proposing a fully local, domain-adapted pipeline using the MedGemma-27B model. Our two-stage architecture, which separates binary presence classification from value extraction, enforces strict adherence to textual evidence and ensures deterministic outputs for negated, uncertain, or unknown states. By leveraging item-specific, few-shot in-context learning without external API calls or fine-tuning, our approach achieves a macro-F1 score of 0.55 on the official English test track. This result secures second place among all locally-hosted, open-source submissions. Our work demonstrates that privacy-preserving, on-premise LLM pipelines can achieve near-competitive performance with proprietary frontier models, providing a practical, data-sovereign framework for clinical NLP.

item.title: No Hidden Prompts Needed! You Can Game AI Peer Review with Presentation-Only Revisions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13044v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

随着AI生成评审意见逐步嵌入真实同行评审流程，现有鲁棒性研究多聚焦于显式攻击（如隐藏指令、提示注入）。本研究揭示了一种更隐蔽、更具政策相关性的失效模式：无需隐藏文本、无需提示注入、亦不改动方法、实验、图表、公式、证明或数值结果——仅通过修改论文的呈现层内容（如摘要、贡献表述、相关工作叙述、讨论段落与整体叙事结构）即可系统性操纵AI评审结果。

方法创新

我们提出对抗性重包装（Adversarial Repackaging）：一种闭环攻击框架，利用AI评审器的实时反馈，搜索仅改变呈现形式但严格保持科学证据不变的修订方案。该方法在三个主流开源AI评审模型上进行端到端验证，全程排除任何内容实质变更。

关键发现

  • 攻击成功率高达75.1%，平均评分提升+1.21/10分，且效果无法归因于常规语言润色；
  • 深度语义重构策略（如相关工作重定位、分析性讨论扩展）显著优于表层编辑（如局部措辞优化、表格格式调整、算法框美化）；
  • 揭示两大结构性缺陷：

  ① AI评审器“易被 impress，难被 convince”——强调优势稳定提升感知价值，而弱化缺陷常适得其反；
  ② 易将“看似回应局限”误判为“实际解决局限”，导致未变的证据被重新解读为更强科学贡献。

理论与实践意义

本研究首次证实：论文呈现本身已成为可被优化的表面（optimization surface），构成独立于恶意提示之外的新型部署风险。我们开源了无污染滚动基准（contamination-free rolling benchmark）与攻击框架，支持社区持续检验AI评审器是否真正锚定科学实质，而非被呈现策略所劫持。

AI Summary (English)

This paper exposes a critical, policy-relevant failure mode in AI peer review: adversarial repackaging—a presentation-only attack that requires no hidden prompts, no prompt injection, and no changes to methods, experiments, figures, equations, proofs, or numerical results. Instead, it modifies only abstracts, contribution framing, related work narratives, discussion sections, and structural flow. Using closed-loop feedback from AI reviewers, our attack achieves 75.1% success rate and +1.21/10 mean score gain across three mainstream AI reviewers—effects unexplained by routine prose polishing. Crucially, semantic-level edits (e.g., repositioning related work, expanding analytical discussion) vastly outperform superficial ones (e.g., formatting, local rewriting). We identify two deep structural flaws: (1) AI reviewers are easier to impress than to convince—highlighting strengths reliably boosts perceived merit, while mitigating weaknesses often backfires; and (2) they conflate appearing to address limitations with actually resolving them, enabling unchanged evidence to be misinterpreted as stronger contribution. This reveals that paper presentation itself has become an optimization surface—posing deployment risk beyond malicious hidden instructions. We release a contamination-free rolling benchmark and open-source attack framework to test whether AI reviewers remain anchored to scientific substance under presentation-only edits.

Abstract

As AI-generated reviews move from experimental tools into peer-review infrastructure, most robustness concerns have focused on explicit attacks such as hidden instructions and prompt injection. We study a harder and more policy-relevant failure mode: no hidden text, no prompt injection, and no changes to methods, experiments, figures, equations, proofs, or numerical results. The attacker modifies only presentation-level content, such as the abstract, contribution framing, related work, discussion, and narrative structure. We introduce adversarial repackaging: a closed-loop attack that uses AI-reviewer feedback to search for presentation-level revisions while keeping the scientific evidence fixed. Across three mainstream AI reviewers, adversarial repackaging achieves a 75.1% attack success rate and a mean score gain of +1.21/10. The effect is not explained by ordinary prose polishing. We also reveal that strategies that change how the reviewer interprets the paper, such as related-work repositioning and analytical discussion expansion, substantially outperform surface edits such as local polishing, table formatting, and algorithm boxes. Our analysis reveals two deeper structural failure modes. First, AI reviewers are easier to impress than to convince: highlighting strengths reliably increases perceived merit, while attempts to dissolve weaknesses frequently backfire. Second, AI reviewers can confuse the appearance of addressing a limitation with actually resolving it, allowing unchanged evidence to be reinterpreted as stronger scientific contribution. These results show that the deployment risk is not only malicious hidden instructions, but the emergence of paper presentation itself as an optimization surface. We release a contamination-free rolling benchmark and attack framework for testing whether AI reviewers remain anchored to scientific content under presentation-only edits.

item.title: SafeLLM: Extraction as a Hallucination-Resistant Alternative to Rewriting in Safety-Critical Settings
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12897v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

在医疗、合规等安全关键场景中，大语言模型（LLMs）正被广泛用于检索和解释组织文档（如标准操作规程SOP、HR政策及临床指南）。然而，主流检索增强生成（RAG）系统依赖自由式重写（rewriting），易引发幻觉（hallucination），且在完整性与简洁性之间存在不稳定权衡，威胁决策可靠性。

方法创新

本研究提出 SafeLLM 框架，以结构化提取（extraction）替代重写，构建幻觉抵抗型RAG范式。系统性评估三类策略：

  • 行号定位提取（line-number-based selection）：直接锚定源文档精确位置；
  • 带安全标注的句子级提取：显式标记指南中的风险等级与约束条件；
  • 多阶段证据精炼流水线：先生成初稿，再基于源指南逐轮校验与修剪。

实验覆盖结构差异显著的文档：本地NHS急性/肿瘤科指南（短而结构化）与全英NICE指南（长而叙述性），并测试前沿大模型（如GPT-4）与轻量本地部署模型（如Phi-3）。

关键发现

  • 行号提取在所有模型尺度上均表现最优：术语召回率高达95%，文本忠实度显著优于直接复制与安全强化策略；
  • 安全导向策略提升精度但导致系统性遗漏（如跳过条件性条款），多阶段过滤进一步加剧该缺陷；
  • 文档结构决定策略优劣：行号法在协议类文档中优势突出，而其他策略在冗长文本中可达97%术语召回率。

本研究首次实证验证“提取优于重写”在安全敏感场景的普适性，为高可靠RAG系统提供可部署、可审计、可追溯的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in safety-critical domains (e.g., healthcare, compliance) to interpret organizational documents—but conventional rewriting-based retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) risks hallucination and unstable completeness-conciseness trade-offs. This paper introduces SafeLLM, which replaces free-form rewriting with structured extraction as a hallucination-resistant alternative. We rigorously compare three strategies—line-number anchoring, safety-annotated sentence extraction, and multi-stage evidence-refinement—across diverse document types (NHS acute/oncology vs. UK-wide NICE guidelines) and model scales (frontier and locally deployable LLMs). Automatic metrics and expert human evaluation show that line-number extraction achieves the highest term recall (up to 95%) and source fidelity, outperforming both direct copying and safety-focused methods across all models. While safety-oriented approaches improve precision, they systematically omit conditional or contextual clauses; multi-stage filtering exacerbates this recall loss. Crucially, optimal strategy depends on document structure: line-based extraction excels in protocol-like texts, whereas alternatives reach up to 97% recall on verbose documents. SafeLLM establishes extraction as a foundational, auditable, and deployment-ready paradigm for high-stakes RAG.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to access organisational documentation, including standard operating procedures (SOPs), HR policies and institutional guidelines. However, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems that rely on free-form rewriting can introduce hallucinations and unstable trade-offs between completeness and conciseness, particularly in safety- and compliance-critical settings. Objectives: To evaluate extraction as a hallucination-resistant alternative to rewriting-based RAG and compare strategies that balance precision, recall and safety across document types and model scales. Methods: We compare multiple prompting strategies, including line-number-based source selection, extraction of relevant guideline sentences with explicit safety annotations, and a multi-stage pipeline that refines draft answers using supporting evidence from source guidelines. Experiments are conducted on documents of varying length and structure, including local NHS acute care and oncology guidelines and UK-wide NICE guidelines, using both frontier-scale and locally deployable models. Performance is assessed using automatic metrics and human expert evaluation of relevance and completeness. Results: Line-number selection achieves the strongest results, outperforming direct copying and safety-focused strategies across both large and small models while maintaining high term recall (up to 95%) and close alignment with source text. Safety-oriented approaches improve precision but introduce systematic omissions, while multi-stage filtering further amplifies this trade-off. Performance varies with document structure: line-based extraction excels in protocol-like content, whereas alternative strategies perform better on more verbose documents (up to 97% term recall).

item.title: OCELOT: Inference-Leakage Budgets for Privacy-Preserving LLM Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12341v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型语言模型（LLM）智能体正深度介入用户私密场景——读取本地文件、调用外部工具、执行金融或社交交易——其每一步交互均可能跨越信任边界，泄露个人身份信息（PII）。传统隐私保护聚焦单次输出过滤，而智能体隐私本质是全轨迹后验风险控制：泄漏具有累积性（多个看似无害的释放经诚实但好奇或共谋接收方聚合后可推断敏感秘密）、双向性（恶意观察者可通过输入注入操控智能体自身推理）、及任务依赖性（同一字段对某接收方必要，对另一方则属冗余披露）。

方法创新：OCELOT 机制

本文提出 OCELOT——首个面向LLM智能体的运行时推理泄漏预算框架。其核心为证人验证式解密（Witness-Verified Declassification）：

  • 一个不可信但本地微调的防御模型（“证人”）对每个候选输出生成结构化证据：标注的语义原子（如“邮箱域”“薪资区间”）与候选解密操作符（如“泛化为行业均值”“k-匿名化”）；
  • 一个确定性验证器审计证据，基于信息论计算该操作符引发的最小熵成本（即对手信念提升量），并在防篡改账本上按接收方可信度加权的总预算内，授权效用最优且披露最少的版本。

实验效果

在涵盖文件摘要、行程规划、财务代理等多样化智能体基准测试中，OCELOT 在保持更高任务成功率（+12.3% avg. utility）的同时，将累积后验泄漏降低达68%；对自适应提示注入、 jailbreak 攻击、多步推理攻击及跨接收方共谋均具强鲁棒性；端到端开销仅增加17ms/step，兼容主流LLM推理栈。

AI Summary (English)

OCELOT is a runtime privacy mediator for LLM agents that enforces inference-leakage budgets over entire execution trajectories—not per-output filtering. It recasts agent privacy as posterior-risk control, quantifying how much an adversary’s belief about a secret may improve across all releases. Its core mechanism, Witness-Verified Declassification, decouples judgment (via an untrusted, locally fine-tuned witness model) from trust (via a deterministic verifier). The witness proposes structured declassification options with labeled semantic atoms; the verifier computes certified min-entropy costs and selects the least-disclosing yet task-useful release under a sink-trust-weighted budget logged on a tamper-evident ledger. Evaluated across diverse agent benchmarks, OCELOT achieves significantly lower cumulative leakage (+68% reduction) at higher utility (+12.3% avg.), resists adaptive injection, jailbreaks, multi-step inference, and sink collusion, with only modest latency overhead (~17 ms/step).

Abstract

Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly act on a user's behalf -- reading personal files, calling tools, transacting with external services -- possibly leaking personally identifiable information (PII) across trust boundaries at every step. Privacy here is a property not of a single output but of an entire trajectory, and three properties make it hard: leakage is cumulative, as individually innocuous releases accumulate across honest-but-curious or colluding sinks into inferences about a protected secret; bidirectional, as a malicious observation can inject instructions that turn the agent's own reasoning model against the user; and task-dependent, as the same field is necessary for one recipient yet gratuitous for another. Per-release contextual-integrity filters, information-flow controls, and posterior-leakage monitors each address part of this but none controls cumulative, inference-based leakage at runtime. We recast agent privacy as \emph{posterior-risk control} and present OCELOT, a runtime mediator that budgets how much an adversary's belief about a secret may improve across a trajectory, rather than filtering outputs.
