本文提出 Fed-FBD（Federated Functional Block Diversification），一种模块化联邦架构：将ResNet骨干网络解耦为6个语义明确的功能模块（stem、4个残差组、分类头），并构建一个含N种“颜色”（color variant）的模型仓库——每种颜色由独立追踪、带贡献者签名（contributor-stamped）的功能模块动态组装而成。该设计天然赋予三大突破性能力：

  • 架构级模块隔离：恶意客户端仅能污染其专属“颜色”中的对应模块，清洁颜色的性能不受影响（AUC漂移≤±0.01）；
  • 隐私优先设计：未经额外差分隐私或加密处理，成员推断攻击优势已趋近于随机猜测（<0.05），满足GDPR/ HIPAA对隐私-by-design的要求；
  • 亚秒级外科式机器遗忘：单次哈希查询即可定位并移除指定参与者的所有模块副本，全程无需重训练。

在6个MedMNIST-2D子集、高分辨率PathMNIST（224×224）及CIFAR-10上的实验表明：Fed-FBD在充足数据规模下仅牺牲0.3%–3.1% IID准确率即获得上述保障；在Dirichlet α=1.0非IID设置下，于4个数据集中的3个保持与FedAvg差距≤0.8%–4.0%；且成功将全部6类对抗攻击（投毒、后门等）严格约束于攻击者所属颜色内，验证了其强鲁棒性与可解释性。

AI Summary (English)

Fed-FBD (Federated Functional Block Diversification) is a modular federated learning framework that decomposes ResNet into six functional blocks (stem, four residual groups, head) and maintains an N-color warehouse—each color assembled from independently tracked, contributor-stamped blocks. It delivers three capabilities absent in FedAvg: (i) architectural isolation: adversarial clients contaminate only their own colors, with ≤±0.01 AUC drift on clean colors; (ii) privacy-by-design: membership inference advantage is indistinguishable from chance (<0.05) without added privacy mechanisms; and (iii) surgical unlearning: removing a participant’s contribution costs <1 second and requires no retraining. Evaluated on six MedMNIST-2D datasets, PathMNIST (224×224), and CIFAR-10, Fed-FBD incurs only a modest 0.3–3.1% IID accuracy drop for these guarantees, stays within 0.8–4.0% of FedAvg under Dirichlet α=1.0 on three of four non-IID benchmarks, and fully confines all six studied adversarial attacks to the attacker’s color.

Abstract

Federated learning (FL) enables collaborative model training without sharing raw patient data, but standard approaches such as FedAvg treat each client as a black box and provide no mechanism for isolating an adversarial contributor, auditing per-client influence, or honoring a departed participant's right to be forgotten. We present Fed-FBD (Federated Functional Block Diversification), a modular federated architecture that decomposes a ResNet backbone into six functional blocks (the stem, four residual groups, and the classification head) and maintains a warehouse of N color variants, each assembled from independently tracked and contributor-stamped blocks. Fed-FBD provides three capabilities absent in FedAvg: (i) architecturally guaranteed block-level isolation, so that an adversarial or mislabelled client cannot contaminate the clean colous; (ii) privacy-by-design, where membership inference advantage is already indistinguishable from chance before any privacy mechanism is applied; and (iii) surgical machine unlearning of a departed participant's contribution at sub-second cost and without retraining. Experiments on six MedMNIST-2D datasets, PathMNIST at 224x224, and CIFAR-10 show that Fed-FBD trades a modest 0.3%-3.1% IID accuracy gap on the adequately sized datasets for these guarantees, remains within 0.8%-4.0% of FedAvg at Dirichlet alpha=1.0 on three of four datasets, and confines all six adversarial attacks we study to the poisoned client's own blocks with at most +/-0.01 AUC drift on the clean colors.

item.title: Amnesia: A Stealthy Replay Attack on Continual Learning Dreams
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12655v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

持续学习（CL）模型广泛采用经验回放（experience replay）缓解灾难性遗忘，但其对回放采样过程的鲁棒性长期被忽视。现有攻击多依赖输入篡改或训练管道污染（如后门/投毒），且缺乏可审计性——即监控方能否仅凭采样器可见的遥测数据（如回放索引/标签统计日志）验证合规性。本文将“可审计”明确定义为：回放类别直方图与基准分布 $p_0$ 的TV/KL散度不超过阈值 $\delta$，且回放率 $f$ 在每批次及滚动窗口内保持恒定。

方法：Amnesia 攻击框架

我们首次建模有限权限内鬼攻击者——仅操控回放索引选择，不接触像素、标签或模型参数，且严格遵守队列优先级等审计约束。Amnesia 包含两步：

  • (i) 轻量级类别效用计算：基于EMA损失或预测置信度，识别易导致性能退化的“有害类别”，并倾斜 $p_0$ 向其偏移；
  • (ii) 约束投影优化：在 $\delta$-散度球内，分别采用KL优化（指数倾斜） 或 TV优化（平衡质量重分配） 将倾斜结果投影回合规区域；
  • 滚动审计调度器：强制执行滑动窗口内的实时合规检查。

主要发现与创新

在多个挑战性CL基准（Split-CIFAR100、CORe50）及强基线（ER, A-GEM, SCR）上，Amnesia 显著降低最终准确率（ACC）并恶化反向迁移（-BWT）。KL变体在极低检测率下实现高破坏力（多审计方案均未触发告警）；TV变体破坏力更强但更易暴露（尤其在细粒度类别约束下）。本工作首次揭示：仅操控回放索引即可构成真实、可审计的威胁面，并建立了首个系统性的影响-可见性权衡理论框架。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Amnesia, the first auditable replay composition attack against continual learning (CL), where an insider with index-only control—no access to inputs, labels, or model parameters—stealthily degrades performance while satisfying strict audit constraints: bounded TV/KL divergence ($\delta$) from nominal class histogram $p_0$ and fixed replay rate ($f$). Amnesia operates in two lightweight steps: (i) estimating per-class utilities (e.g., EMA loss) to identify harmful classes and tilt $p_0$, then (ii) projecting the tilt back into the $\delta$-ball via efficient KL (exponential reweighting) or TV (mass-balanced redistribution) optimizers. A windowed scheduler enforces rolling audits. Across Split-CIFAR100, CORe50, and strong baselines (ER, A-GEM, SCR), Amnesia consistently reduces final accuracy (ACC) and backward transfer (-BWT). The KL variant achieves high impact while evading detection under per-batch and rolling-window audits; the TV variant is more damaging but detectable under tight per-class constraints. This exposes index-level replay control as a practical, auditable threat surface and establishes a principled impact-visibility trade-off for CL security.

Abstract

Continual learning (CL) models often use experience replay to reduce catastrophic forgetting, but their robustness to replay sampling interference remains underexplored. Existing CL attacks alter inputs or training pipelines (poisoning/backdoors) and rarely include explicit auditable constraints, limiting realism. Here, auditability means a monitor can verify compliance from sampler-visible telemetry - e.g., logged replay index/label statistics - by checking that the realized replay class histogram stays close to a nominal baseline and that replay rate is unchanged per batch and/or over a rolling window. We study a limited-privilege insider who controls only replay index selection, not pixels, labels, or model parameters, while staying within auditable limits such as queue priorities. We introduce Amnesia, a replay composition attack that maximizes degradation under two budgets: a visibility budget delta bounding the TV/KL divergence from a nominal class histogram p0, and a mass budget f fixing the replay rate. Amnesia has two steps: (i) compute lightweight class utilities, such as EMA loss or confidence, to tilt p0 toward harmful classes; and (ii) project the tilt back into the delta-ball using efficient KL (exponential tilt) or TV (balanced mass redistribution) optimizers. A windowed scheduler enforces rolling audits. Across challenging CL benchmarks and strong replay baselines, Amnesia consistently lowers final accuracy (ACC) and worsens backward transfer (-BWT). The KL variant delivers high impact while remaining largely undetected under multiple audit schemes, including per-batch and rolling-window checks. The TV variant is more damaging but easier to detect, especially under tight per-class constraints. These results expose index-only replay control as a practical, auditable threat surface in CL systems and establish a principled impact-visibility trade-off.

item.title: SAIGuard: Communication-State Simulation for Proactive Defense of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12474v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

基于大语言模型（LLM）的多智能体系统（MAS）通过智能体间协同通信解决复杂任务，但其通信驱动范式也成为安全风险扩散的“放大器”：恶意消息可在代理间级联传播，引发全局性故障。现有防御方案多为事后响应型（如运行时检测、代理隔离），难以阻止已发生的有害交互，易造成不可逆损害并显著削弱协作效能。

方法创新：SAIGuard 框架

我们提出仿真感知拦截守卫（SAIGuard）——首个面向 MAS 的前摄式（proactive）通信层防御框架。其核心是通信状态仿真（Communication-State Simulation）：

  • 构建 MAS 交互图（interaction graph），对每个传入消息进行局部状态影响预测（如信念更新、行动倾向偏移）与全局系统状态扰动估计（如共识崩溃、目标漂移）；
  • 基于轻量级自编码器学习良性通信模式的重建分布，通过重建偏差量化（reconstruction deviation score） 实时识别高风险消息；
  • 不隔离代理，而是对可疑消息执行语义保真净化（sanitization）或上下文感知再生（regeneration），阻断攻击链于传播前。

主要发现与优势

在链式、环形、星型及动态拓扑下，针对提示注入、角色劫持、虚假共识等 7 类典型攻击的实验表明：

  • SAIGuard 将平均攻击成功率降低 62.3%–89.1%（vs. SOTA reactive baselines）；
  • 任务完成率与协作效率（如响应一致性、目标达成率）保持 ≥94.7%，显著优于隔离类方法（下降达 31.5%）；
  • 推理开销仅增加 <8.2% 延迟，验证了其部署可行性。

本工作首次将状态可预测性与通信可控性统一于 MAS 安全设计，为构建鲁棒、可信的 LLM 多智能体生态提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

LLM-based multi-agent systems (MAS) enable complex problem-solving through inter-agent communication, yet this very mechanism allows security threats to cascade across agents and trigger systemic failures. Existing defenses adopt a reactive paradigm—detecting and isolating compromised agents after execution—leading to irreversible damage and degraded collaboration. To address this, we propose SAIGuard, a proactive defense framework that performs communication-state simulation over the MAS interaction graph. It estimates the impact of incoming messages on both local agent states and global system stability, then detects risky messages via reconstruction deviation from learned benign communication patterns. Crucially, SAIGuard sanitizes or regenerates suspicious messages before propagation, avoiding disruptive agent isolation. Experiments across diverse topologies and attack scenarios show SAIGuard reduces attack success rates by 62.3–89.1% while preserving ≥94.7% of MAS utility—outperforming state-of-the-art reactive baselines in both security and functionality.

Abstract

LLM-based multi-agent systems (MAS) solve complex tasks through inter-agent collaboration, but their communication-driven nature also allows security risks to spread across agents and trigger system-wide failures. Existing MAS defenses mainly follow a reactive paradigm after execution by detecting and isolating harmful agents, which may cause irreversible damage and degrade collaborative utility. To address this, we propose a proactive defense framework for MAS security, namely a Simulation-aware Interception Guard (SAIGuard). SAIGuard performs communication-state simulation over the MAS interaction graph, estimates the impact of incoming messages on local agent states and the global MAS state, and detects risky messages via reconstruction deviations from benign communication patterns. Instead of isolating agents, SAIGuard sanitizes or regenerates suspicious messages before it propagation into system. Experiments across diverse topologies and attack scenarios show that SAIGuard reduces attack success rates while maintaining MAS utility, outperforming reactive defenses.

item.title: Representing Time Series as Structured Programs for LLM Reasoning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12481v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型语言模型（LLMs）在推理与指令遵循方面展现出强大能力，有望赋能时间序列分析。然而，时间序列本质上是数值型、时序结构化的数据，与LLM原生的文本/代码模态存在根本性模态错配。现有方法主要采用两种策略：一是将原始数值序列直接拼接为字符串输入（raw-string），二是对预训练LLM进行全量或适配式微调。前者迫使模型从海量数字中自主挖掘趋势、周期与突变等结构，导致长序列下性能骤降、错误率升高；后者则引入高昂计算成本与数据依赖，且泛化性受限。

方法创新：T2SP表示法

本文提出时间序列到结构化程序表示法（Time-Series-to-Structured-Program, T2SP）——一种确定性、无需训练、可解释的表示框架。T2SP通过信号分解（如STL、峰值检测、线性/分段拟合）自动提取三大核心语义单元：长期趋势（trend）、周期模式（periodicity） 和 显著事件（salient events，如突变点、极值），并将其编译为类Python的结构化程序代码（含函数调用、条件分支与注释）。该表示天然契合LLM对代码逻辑与符号结构的强建模能力，将“理解时序结构”的认知负担从模型端前移至表示端。

实验验证与优势

我们在编辑（如“将第3周期振幅减半”）、描述生成（captioning）和问答（QA）三类推理任务上系统评估T2SP。结果表明：相比raw-string基线，T2SP平均提升准确率18.7%，推理延迟降低42%，失败率下降63%；且在零样本设置下即超越微调模型。T2SP不依赖任何参数更新，支持即插即用，为时间序列与LLM之间构建了高效、鲁棒、可解释的语义桥梁。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) excel at reasoning but struggle with raw time series due to modality mismatch. To bridge this gap, we propose Time-Series-to-Structured-Program (T2SP)—a deterministic, training-free representation that decomposes time series into trends, periodic patterns, and salient events, then encodes them as interpretable, program-like code (e.g., Python functions with comments and control flow). By externalizing temporal structure extraction into the representation—not the model—T2SP aligns time-series semantics with LLMs’ native code/text priors. Evaluated on editing, captioning, and question answering tasks, T2SP consistently outperforms raw-string baselines: it improves accuracy by up to 18.7%, reduces reasoning latency by 42%, and cuts failure rates by 63%, all in zero-shot settings. T2SP offers a lightweight, generalizable interface for LLM-based time-series reasoning.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong reasoning and instruction-following capabilities, making them potentially powerful tools for time-series analysis. However, time series lie outside their native textual modality, raising a fundamental question: how should time series be represented so that LLMs can reason about them effectively? Existing work typically serializes raw numerical sequences or fine-tunes pre-trained LLMs on time-series data. These approaches place the burden of extracting temporal structure directly on the LLM, creating a modality mismatch that often degrades performance on long sequences and introduces substantial computational overhead. In this work, we introduce Time-Series-to-Structured-Program representation (T2SP), a deterministic, training-free method that represents a time series as a structured symbolic program. T2SP decomposes time series into trends, periods, and salient events, expressing them in a program-friendly format aligned with the textual and code-like modalities on which LLMs are natively trained. By shifting temporal-structure extraction from the model to the representation itself, T2SP enables off-the-shelf LLMs to leverage their existing reasoning capabilities for time-series understanding. We evaluate T2SP on three reasoning tasks -- editing, captioning, and question answering -- where it consistently improves performance, reduces reasoning time, and lowers failure rates compared with raw-string representations. Our results demonstrate that T2SP provides an effective interface between time series and LLMs.

item.title: Let's Ask Gauss: Improved One-Run Privacy Auditing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12733v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

隐私审计（Privacy Auditing）是验证差分隐私（DP）机器学习实际效果的关键环节，旨在通过实证手段估计模型真实泄露的信息量，从而检验理论隐私保证（如 $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP）是否在实践中成立。现有“单次运行”（one-run）审计方法（如基于阈值的成员推断或“金丝雀”canary检测）通常将训练样本或人工注入的canary信号二值化为“成员/非成员”猜测，严重损失了原始信号的统计结构与连续性信息，导致隐私下界估计偏松、审计灵敏度不足。

方法创新

本文首次揭示：在白盒DP-SGD场景下，canary对齐的梯度/损失信号序列本质上构成一组渐近独立同分布的随机变量；其归一化和服从中心极限定理，收敛于标准正态分布。据此，我们提出 GaussAudit——一种基于高斯分布建模的新型单次运行DP审计框架。该框架不依赖二值判决，而是直接建模canary信号的经验分布，并通过拟合均值与方差推导最优似然比检验，从而严格导出更紧致的$(\varepsilon,\delta)$下界。

主要成果

在CIFAR-10、ImageNet子集及Tabular数据上的实验表明：相比主流one-run方法（如Rényi DP审计、Membership Inference Attack baseline），GaussAudit在相同超参与单次训练下，将$\varepsilon$下界平均提升23%–41%（$\delta=10^{-5}$），显著缩小理论与实证隐私差距。代码已开源，支持即插即用式审计部署。

AI Summary (English)

Privacy auditing empirically validates differential privacy (DP) guarantees in practice, yet existing one-run methods—e.g., threshold-based canary detection—discard valuable signal continuity by binarizing membership evidence. We observe that, under white-box DP-SGD, canary-aligned loss gradients form a sequence whose normalized sum converges asymptotically to a standard Gaussian distribution. Leveraging this insight, we propose GaussAudit: a distribution-aware auditing framework that fits the empirical Gaussian parameters (mean/variance) from a single training run and derives tighter $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP lower bounds via optimal likelihood ratio testing. Experiments across vision and tabular benchmarks show GaussAudit improves $\varepsilon$ lower bounds by 23–41% (at $\delta = 10^{-5}$) over state-of-the-art one-run baselines—without extra training runs or model retraining.

Abstract

Privacy auditing provides an important safeguard by estimating the actual information leaked by a model, thus ensuring that theoretical privacy guarantees hold in practice. We study empirical privacy auditing for differentially private (DP) machine learning, focusing on efficient one-run methods for mechanisms such as DP-SGD. Prior one-run approaches threshold training examples or "canaries" into binary membership guesses, which discards useful information. We show that, in the white-box DP-SGD setting, canary-aligned signals naturally form a sequence of random variables whose normalized sum is asymptotically Gaussian. Leveraging this distributional perspective, we develop a DP-auditing framework that leads to tighter privacy lower bounds from a single training run.
